Massachusetts allow patients to live at home while receiving high-quality outpatient care. While the therapies offered are similar to those of inpatient programs, IOP care is slightly lower in intensity. Explore Massachusetts’ IOP offerings, including level of care, length of stay, payment options, and more.  </div><div class="jsx-4001556473 hero__highlights"><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f container"><div style="margin:0 0 15px 0" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f text-sm bold">What you will learn:</div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">IOP basics (e.g., level of care, length of treatment, etc.).</div></div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">How to find a Massachusetts IOP.</div></div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">Payment and insurance options.</div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 container"><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 content"><div id="wordpress-content" class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555 toc__container"><div class="sc-ae1ffb5e-0 emMzuR"><div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div><h2><strong>What is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?</strong></h2>
<p>Offered in rehab facilities, counseling offices, clinics, and more, intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Massachusetts and elsewhere are a type of outpatient rehab. While IOPs provide therapies similar to those offered via inpatient facilities, care is slightly less intense and less frequent, and patients stay in their own homes as opposed to the facility. Conversely, IOPs offer slightly more rigorous treatment than traditional outpatient programs.</p>
<p>IOPs in Massachusetts typically require 6 to 30 hours of treatment per week over the span of at least 90 days.<sup>1,2 </sup> That said, each IOP program is unique, and the <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/levels-of-care" target="_blank" rel="noopener">level of care</a> selected for each individual should be tailored to their specific needs.</p>
<p>Sometimes, patients enter treatment via an IOP, perhaps following detox. Other times, however, IOPs are used to help those formerly in inpatient settings transition to a lower level of care, allowing them to ease into a new normal following intensive treatment. Either way, IOP patients often begin traditional outpatient and/or aftercare programs following the completion of their IOP therapy.</p>
<p>Given the fact that patients live at home, IOPs are typically best suited to individuals that don’t need 24/7 supervision and that have already completed detox if needed.<sup>1</sup> Along with the ongoing motivation to complete treatment, it’s important that IOP patients have transportation to and from therapy as well as a sober home environment.<sup>3</sup></p>
<h2><strong> Where Can You Find an IOP in Massachusetts?</strong></h2>
<p>Massachusetts is home to <a href="https://findtreatment.gov/locator" target="_blank" rel="noopener">398 substance use treatment facilities</a> of which  81 are intensive outpatient programs.<sup>4</sup> Although most rehabs are near major metropolitan areas, such as Boston, Worcester, and Springfield, you’ll find IOP facilities scattered throughout the state.</p>
<p>Keep in mind that <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth/florida" target="_blank" rel="noopener">telehealth</a> may also be an option for some portions of intensive outpatient programs in MA, as 72 of the state’s 81 IOPs indicate that some form of telehealth services are available.<sup>4</sup> Telehealth enlists phone and online communication to connect patients to treatment professionals. Telehealth services usually allow patients to partake in group and individual therapy, medication-assisted treatment (MAT), peer support groups, and more.<sup>5</sup> <sup>  </sup></p>
<p>American Addiction Centers, a leading provider of addiction and mental health care, has multiple outpatient facilities in the Massachusetts area. <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/adcare-hospital" target="_blank" rel="noopener">AdCare outpatient services in Worcester, MA</a>, and multiple AdCare outpatient facilities in Rhode Island offer a host of treatment options including IOPs.</p>
<p>Keep in mind, however, IOPs certainly aren’t the only treatment avenues available. For example, <a href="/treatment-centers" target="_blank" rel="noopener">American Addiction Center facilities</a> offer the gamut of services including:<sup>6</sup></p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Medical detox</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Inpatient</a> or residential rehab.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Outpatient treatment.</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs).</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/partial-hospitalization-programs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Partial hospitalization programs (PHPs).</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/aftercare" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Aftercare.</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Telehealth</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>Contact us via our 24/7 confidential helpline at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a78054a5fd8"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span> to learn more about treatment options, verify insurance benefits, and more.</p>
<h2><strong>What Substances Do IOPs in Massachusetts Treat?</strong></h2>
<p>Some programs focus on specific substances, such as opioids or alcohol, but most IOPs treat a wide range of substance use disorders. For example, the AdCare facilities in Massachusetts and Rhode Island treat disorders related to the following substances and more:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Alcohol</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/benzodiazepine/symptoms-and-signs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Benzodiazepines</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/cocaine/signs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cocaine</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/fentanyl" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Fentanyl</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/heroin/rehab-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Heroin</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/meth/rehab-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Methamphetamine</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Opioids</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/prescription-drugs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Prescription drugs</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>Also note that most facilities offer treatment for polysubstance use, which is when people intentionally or unintentionally use more than one substance (e.g., <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol/mixing-opioids" target="_blank" rel="noopener">alcohol and opioids</a>) within a short period of time.<sup>7</sup></p>
<p>Additionally, 63 of the IOPs in MA also treat for <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders" target="_blank" rel="noopener">co-occurring mental conditions</a>, such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders.<sup>4,8</sup> For those with a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder, concurrent treatment is usually more effective than treating each disorder independently.<sup>6</sup></p>
<h2><strong>How Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in MA Cost?</strong></h2>
<p>The cost of an IOP varies depending on a variety of factors, including the services required, length of treatment, insurance benefits, facility pricing, and more. Even if you have insurance, there are a host of variables that can affect out-of-pocket costs, such as deductibles, co-pays, in-network versus out-of-network provider fees, etc.</p>
<p>That said, however, you can get a much better handle on how much you’ll pay by verifying your insurance benefits. Reach out to AAC at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a78054a5feb"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span>, and an admissions navigator can assist you with this process.</p>
<p>Keep in mind, a lack of insurance doesn’t have to be a barrier to getting the care you need. There are other ways to pay for IOPs as described below.</p>
<h2><strong>Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Massachusetts?</strong></h2>
<p>IOP <a href="/treatment-centers/massachusetts">treatment in Massachusetts</a> and elsewhere is typically covered at least in part by insurance plans thanks to the <a href="https://www.cms.gov/CCIIO/Programs-and-Initiatives/Other-Insurance-Protections/mhpaea_factsheet" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA)</a> and the <a href="https://www.hhs.gov/answers/health-insurance-reform/does-the-aca-cover-individuals-with-mental-health-problems/index.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="broken_link">Affordable Care Act (ACA)</a>.<sup>9,10  </sup>These laws basically guarantee that insurance providers offer equitable coverage for physical health and mental health services.</p>
<p>Government- and military-funded insurance plans also offer full or partial coverage for substance use treatment such as IOPs. Among Massachusetts’ 81 IOPs, the following number of facilities accept these respective insurance types:<sup>4</sup> <sup> </sup></p>
<ul>
<li>56: <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/using-medicaid-to-pay-for-rehab" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Medicaid</a>.</li>
<li>33: <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/tricare" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Tricare</a>.</li>
<li>42: <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/medicare" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Medicare</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>Insurance, however, certainly isn’t the only way to pay for treatment. Most facilities accept <a href="/payment-options/private-pay" target="_blank" rel="noopener">private payment (aka self-pay)</a>. However, other common payment options include:<sup>4,11</sup> <sup> </sup></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Payment assistance. </strong>Seven intensive outpatient programs in Massachusetts indicate that they offer payment assistance for eligible low-income, uninsured, and underinsured patients.</li>
<li><strong>Sliding fee scales.</strong> Sliding fee scales, where prices are based on the patient’s ability to pay for treatment, are offered at 15 IOPs in Massachusetts.</li>
<li><strong>Grants and scholarships.</strong> Currently 8 IOPs in Massachusetts accept block grant funding via the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA).</li>
<li><strong>Indian Health Service (IHS)/Tribal/Urban—ITU Funds. </strong>Seven IOPs indicate that they accept ITU funds, which are part of the Indian healthcare system.</li>
<li><strong>Free and state-funded programs. </strong>Like some other states, Massachusetts may also offers a handful of <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free" target="_blank" rel="noopener">free</a> and/or <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/state-funded" target="_blank" rel="noopener">state-funded programs</a>, which are available to eligible individuals based on their special population status, income, and more.</li>
</ul>
<p>If you or a loved one is struggling with substance use, <a href="/treatment-centers" target="_blank" rel="noopener">American Addiction Centers</a> can help. Call our confidential helpline at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a78054a5ff3"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span> to speak to one of our admissions navigators, who can answer your questions about IOPs and myriad other levels of care. Along with verifying your insurance benefits, they can also help identify other payment options and aid you in taking the first steps toward recovery.</p>

</script><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"_sentryTraceData":"b795d5247e5244a7a00d20aa5a67d349-85d6a7cd90b82866-0","_sentryBaggage":"sentry-environment=production,sentry-release=odsUSnXHa7Xks8o0SB39f,sentry-public_key=96a2ce264cf34a8eb5bbd664e8a614e8,sentry-trace_id=b795d5247e5244a7a00d20aa5a67d349,sentry-sample_rate=0.01,sentry-transaction=%2F%5B...pathname%5D,sentry-sampled=false","data":{"id":102237,"date":"2023-06-22T10:03:08","modified":"2025-03-17T14:30:46","slug":"massachusetts","type":"page","link":"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/massachusetts","title":{"rendered":"Intensive Outpatient Programs in Massachusetts: Finding IOP Programs in MA"},"content":{"rendered":"\u003cdiv\u003e\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eOffered in rehab facilities, counseling offices, clinics, and more, intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Massachusetts and elsewhere are a type of outpatient rehab. While IOPs provide therapies similar to those offered via inpatient facilities, care is slightly less intense and less frequent, and patients stay in their own homes as opposed to the facility. Conversely, IOPs offer slightly more rigorous treatment than traditional outpatient programs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIOPs in Massachusetts typically require 6 to 30 hours of treatment per week over the span of at least 90 days.\u003csup\u003e1,2 \u003c/sup\u003e That said, each IOP program is unique, and the \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/levels-of-care\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003elevel of care\u003c/a\u003e selected for each individual should be tailored to their specific needs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eSometimes, patients enter treatment via an IOP, perhaps following detox. Other times, however, IOPs are used to help those formerly in inpatient settings transition to a lower level of care, allowing them to ease into a new normal following intensive treatment. Either way, IOP patients often begin traditional outpatient and/or aftercare programs following the completion of their IOP therapy.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eGiven the fact that patients live at home, IOPs are typically best suited to individuals that don’t need 24/7 supervision and that have already completed detox if needed.\u003csup\u003e1\u003c/sup\u003e Along with the ongoing motivation to complete treatment, it’s important that IOP patients have transportation to and from therapy as well as a sober home environment.\u003csup\u003e3\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e Where Can You Find an IOP in Massachusetts?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eMassachusetts is home to \u003ca href=\"https://findtreatment.gov/locator\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003e398 substance use treatment facilities\u003c/a\u003e of which  81 are intensive outpatient programs.\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e Although most rehabs are near major metropolitan areas, such as Boston, Worcester, and Springfield, you’ll find IOP facilities scattered throughout the state.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eKeep in mind that \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth/florida\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003etelehealth\u003c/a\u003e may also be an option for some portions of intensive outpatient programs in MA, as 72 of the state’s 81 IOPs indicate that some form of telehealth services are available.\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e Telehealth enlists phone and online communication to connect patients to treatment professionals. Telehealth services usually allow patients to partake in group and individual therapy, medication-assisted treatment (MAT), peer support groups, and more.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e \u003csup\u003e  \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAmerican Addiction Centers, a leading provider of addiction and mental health care, has multiple outpatient facilities in the Massachusetts area. \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/adcare-hospital\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAdCare outpatient services in Worcester, MA\u003c/a\u003e, and multiple AdCare outpatient facilities in Rhode Island offer a host of treatment options including IOPs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eKeep in mind, however, IOPs certainly aren’t the only treatment avenues available. For example, \u003ca href=\"/treatment-centers\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAmerican Addiction Center facilities\u003c/a\u003e offer the gamut of services including:\u003csup\u003e6\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMedical detox\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eInpatient\u003c/a\u003e or residential rehab.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eOutpatient treatment.\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eIntensive outpatient programs (IOPs).\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/partial-hospitalization-programs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003ePartial hospitalization programs (PHPs).\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/aftercare\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAftercare.\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eTelehealth\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eContact us via our 24/7 confidential helpline at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a78054a5fd8\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e to learn more about treatment options, verify insurance benefits, and more.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat Substances Do IOPs in Massachusetts Treat?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eSome programs focus on specific substances, such as opioids or alcohol, but most IOPs treat a wide range of substance use disorders. For example, the AdCare facilities in Massachusetts and Rhode Island treat disorders related to the following substances and more:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAlcohol\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/benzodiazepine/symptoms-and-signs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eBenzodiazepines\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/cocaine/signs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eCocaine\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/fentanyl\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eFentanyl\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/heroin/rehab-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eHeroin\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/meth/rehab-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMethamphetamine\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eOpioids\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/prescription-drugs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003ePrescription drugs\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAlso note that most facilities offer treatment for polysubstance use, which is when people intentionally or unintentionally use more than one substance (e.g., \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol/mixing-opioids\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003ealcohol and opioids\u003c/a\u003e) within a short period of time.\u003csup\u003e7\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAdditionally, 63 of the IOPs in MA also treat for \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eco-occurring mental conditions\u003c/a\u003e, such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders.\u003csup\u003e4,8\u003c/sup\u003e For those with a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder, concurrent treatment is usually more effective than treating each disorder independently.\u003csup\u003e6\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHow Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in MA Cost?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThe cost of an IOP varies depending on a variety of factors, including the services required, length of treatment, insurance benefits, facility pricing, and more. Even if you have insurance, there are a host of variables that can affect out-of-pocket costs, such as deductibles, co-pays, in-network versus out-of-network provider fees, etc.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThat said, however, you can get a much better handle on how much you’ll pay by verifying your insurance benefits. Reach out to AAC at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a78054a5feb\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e, and an admissions navigator can assist you with this process.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eKeep in mind, a lack of insurance doesn’t have to be a barrier to getting the care you need. There are other ways to pay for IOPs as described below.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDoes Insurance Cover IOPs in Massachusetts?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIOP \u003ca href=\"/treatment-centers/massachusetts\"\u003etreatment in Massachusetts\u003c/a\u003e and elsewhere is typically covered at least in part by insurance plans thanks to the \u003ca href=\"https://www.cms.gov/CCIIO/Programs-and-Initiatives/Other-Insurance-Protections/mhpaea_factsheet\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA)\u003c/a\u003e and the \u003ca href=\"https://www.hhs.gov/answers/health-insurance-reform/does-the-aca-cover-individuals-with-mental-health-problems/index.html\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" class=\"broken_link\"\u003eAffordable Care Act (ACA)\u003c/a\u003e.\u003csup\u003e9,10  \u003c/sup\u003eThese laws basically guarantee that insurance providers offer equitable coverage for physical health and mental health services.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eGovernment- and military-funded insurance plans also offer full or partial coverage for substance use treatment such as IOPs. Among Massachusetts’ 81 IOPs, the following number of facilities accept these respective insurance types:\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e \u003csup\u003e \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e56: \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/using-medicaid-to-pay-for-rehab\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMedicaid\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e33: \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/tricare\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eTricare\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e42: \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/medicare\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMedicare\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eInsurance, however, certainly isn’t the only way to pay for treatment. Most facilities accept \u003ca href=\"/payment-options/private-pay\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eprivate payment (aka self-pay)\u003c/a\u003e. However, other common payment options include:\u003csup\u003e4,11\u003c/sup\u003e \u003csup\u003e \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePayment assistance. \u003c/strong\u003eSeven intensive outpatient programs in Massachusetts indicate that they offer payment assistance for eligible low-income, uninsured, and underinsured patients.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSliding fee scales.\u003c/strong\u003e Sliding fee scales, where prices are based on the patient’s ability to pay for treatment, are offered at 15 IOPs in Massachusetts.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGrants and scholarships.\u003c/strong\u003e Currently 8 IOPs in Massachusetts accept block grant funding via the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIndian Health Service (IHS)/Tribal/Urban—ITU Funds. \u003c/strong\u003eSeven IOPs indicate that they accept ITU funds, which are part of the Indian healthcare system.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFree and state-funded programs. \u003c/strong\u003eLike some other states, Massachusetts may also offers a handful of \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003efree\u003c/a\u003e and/or \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/state-funded\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003estate-funded programs\u003c/a\u003e, which are available to eligible individuals based on their special population status, income, and more.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIf you or a loved one is struggling with substance use, \u003ca href=\"/treatment-centers\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAmerican Addiction Centers\u003c/a\u003e can help. Call our confidential helpline at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a78054a5ff3\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e to speak to one of our admissions navigators, who can answer your questions about IOPs and myriad other levels of care. Along with verifying your insurance benefits, they can also help identify other payment options and aid you in taking the first steps toward recovery.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_sources\" id=\"portal_sources-69a78054a6114\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003col\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSubstance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration. Intensive Outpatient Programs in Massachusetts: Finding IOP Programs in MA

 Written by: Anna Spooner 

 Edited by: Linda Armstrong 

Updated Mar 17, 2025

 3 min read · 5 sections 

 
 
 
 Medical Detox 
 Insurance Accepted 
 Same Day Admission 

Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Massachusetts allow patients to live at home while receiving high-quality outpatient care. While the therapies offered are similar to those of inpatient programs, IOP care is slightly lower in intensity. Explore Massachusetts’ IOP offerings, including level of care, length of stay, payment options, and more. 

What you will learn:

 
 
IOP basics (e.g., level of care, length of treatment, etc.).

 
 
How to find a Massachusetts IOP.

 
 
Payment and insurance options.

 What is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)? 

Offered in rehab facilities, counseling offices, clinics, and more, intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Massachusetts and elsewhere are a type of outpatient rehab. While IOPs provide therapies similar to those offered via inpatient facilities, care is slightly less intense and less frequent, and patients stay in their own homes as opposed to the facility. Conversely, IOPs offer slightly more rigorous treatment than traditional outpatient programs.

IOPs in Massachusetts typically require 6 to 30 hours of treatment per week over the span of at least 90 days. 1,2  That said, each IOP program is unique, and the level of care selected for each individual should be tailored to their specific needs.

Sometimes, patients enter treatment via an IOP, perhaps following detox. Other times, however, IOPs are used to help those formerly in inpatient settings transition to a lower level of care, allowing them to ease into a new normal following intensive treatment. Either way, IOP patients often begin traditional outpatient and/or aftercare programs following the completion of their IOP therapy.

Given the fact that patients live at home, IOPs are typically best suited to individuals that don’t need 24/7 supervision and that have already completed detox if needed. 1 Along with the ongoing motivation to complete treatment, it’s important that IOP patients have transportation to and from therapy as well as a sober home environment. 3 

 Where Can You Find an IOP in Massachusetts? 

Massachusetts is home to 398 substance use treatment facilities of which  81 are intensive outpatient programs. 4 Although most rehabs are near major metropolitan areas, such as Boston, Worcester, and Springfield, you’ll find IOP facilities scattered throughout the state.

Keep in mind that telehealth may also be an option for some portions of intensive outpatient programs in MA, as 72 of the state’s 81 IOPs indicate that some form of telehealth services are available. 4 Telehealth enlists phone and online communication to connect patients to treatment professionals. Telehealth services usually allow patients to partake in group and individual therapy, medication-assisted treatment (MAT), peer support groups, and more. 5    

American Addiction Centers, a leading provider of addiction and mental health care, has multiple outpatient facilities in the Massachusetts area. AdCare outpatient services in Worcester, MA , and multiple AdCare outpatient facilities in Rhode Island offer a host of treatment options including IOPs.

Keep in mind, however, IOPs certainly aren’t the only treatment avenues available. For example, American Addiction Center facilities offer the gamut of services including: 6 

 

 Medical detox .

 Inpatient or residential rehab.

 Outpatient treatment. 

 Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs). 

 Partial hospitalization programs (PHPs). 

 Aftercare. 

 Telehealth .

 

Contact us via our 24/7 confidential helpline at to learn more about treatment options, verify insurance benefits, and more.

 What Substances Do IOPs in Massachusetts Treat? 

Some programs focus on specific substances, such as opioids or alcohol, but most IOPs treat a wide range of substance use disorders. For example, the AdCare facilities in Massachusetts and Rhode Island treat disorders related to the following substances and more:

 

 Alcohol .

 Benzodiazepines .

 Cocaine .

 Fentanyl .

 Heroin .

 Methamphetamine .

 Opioids .

 Prescription drugs .

 

Also note that most facilities offer treatment for polysubstance use, which is when people intentionally or unintentionally use more than one substance (e.g., alcohol and opioids ) within a short period of time. 7 

Additionally, 63 of the IOPs in MA also treat for co-occurring mental conditions , such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders. 4,8 For those with a substance use disorder and a mental health disorder, concurrent treatment is usually more effective than treating each disorder independently. 6 

 How Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in MA Cost? 

The cost of an IOP varies depending on a variety of factors, including the services required, length of treatment, insurance benefits, facility pricing, and more. Even if you have insurance, there are a host of variables that can affect out-of-pocket costs, such as deductibles, co-pays, in-network versus out-of-network provider fees, etc.

That said, however, you can get a much better handle on how much you’ll pay by verifying your insurance benefits. Reach out to AAC at , and an admissions navigator can assist you with this process.

Keep in mind, a lack of insurance doesn’t have to be a barrier to getting the care you need. There are other ways to pay for IOPs as described below.

 Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Massachusetts? 

IOP treatment in Massachusetts and elsewhere is typically covered at least in part by insurance plans thanks to the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) and the Affordable Care Act (ACA) . 9,10  These laws basically guarantee that insurance providers offer equitable coverage for physical health and mental health services.

Government- and military-funded insurance plans also offer full or partial coverage for substance use treatment such as IOPs. Among Massachusetts’ 81 IOPs, the following number of facilities accept these respective insurance types: 4   

 

56: Medicaid .

33: Tricare .

42: Medicare .

 

Insurance, however, certainly isn’t the only way to pay for treatment. Most facilities accept private payment (aka self-pay) . However, other common payment options include: 4,11   

 

 Payment assistance. Seven intensive outpatient programs in Massachusetts indicate that they offer payment assistance for eligible low-income, uninsured, and underinsured patients.

 Sliding fee scales. Sliding fee scales, where prices are based on the patient’s ability to pay for treatment, are offered at 15 IOPs in Massachusetts.

 Grants and scholarships. Currently 8 IOPs in Massachusetts accept block grant funding via the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA).

 Indian Health Service (IHS)/Tribal/Urban—ITU Funds. Seven IOPs indicate that they accept ITU funds, which are part of the Indian healthcare system.

 Free and state-funded programs. Like some other states, Massachusetts may also offers a handful of free and/or state-funded programs , which are available to eligible individuals based on their special population status, income, and more.

 

If you or a loved one is struggling with substance use, American Addiction Centers can help. Call our confidential helpline at to speak to one of our admissions navigators, who can answer your questions about IOPs and myriad other levels of care. Along with verifying your insurance benefits, they can also help identify other payment options and aid you in taking the first steps toward recovery.

		-webkit-animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
		animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent {
		overflow: hidden;
		position: relative;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .spinner,
	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .bar {
		position: absolute;
	}
	.bcFloat,
	.widget-floating__teaser {
		display: none !important;
	}
	@-webkit-keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}
	@keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}

setting, allowing patients to live in their own homes during rehab. However, therapies at Florida IOPs are similar to inpatient facilities; they’re just provided at a slightly lower intensity. Learn more about Florida IOPs including typical treatment durations, level of care provided, and payment options.</div><div class="jsx-4001556473 hero__highlights"><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f container"><div style="margin:0 0 15px 0" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f text-sm bold">What you will learn:</div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">IOP basics (e.g., level of care, length of treatment, etc.).</div></div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">How to locate a Florida IOP.</div></div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">Insurance coverage and payment options. </div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 container"><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 content"><div id="wordpress-content" class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555 toc__container"><div class="sc-ae1ffb5e-0 emMzuR"><div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div><h2>What is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?</h2>
<p>In Florida and elsewhere, intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) are a form of outpatient treatment for substance use disorders. Provided in clinics, rehab facilities, counseling offices, and more, IOPs in Florida offer therapies similar to that of inpatient facilities. However, this care is provided at a slightly lower intensity, and patients return to their homes at night—rather than staying in the facility 24/7.</p>
<p><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Intensive outpatient programs</a>, which are more rigorous than traditional <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">outpatient treatment</a>, can be entry points to treatment (potentially following detox) for some patients. For others, IOPs are a step down from <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient" target="_blank" rel="noopener">inpatient programs</a>, as they help patients to transition successfully from inpatient rehab into the new normal of everyday life.</p>
<p>While IOPs vary in terms of levels of intensity and participation requirements, many IOP programs in Florida require 6 to 30 hours per week of treatment and last for 90 days.<sup>1,2 </sup>Often these programs are followed by traditional outpatient care and/or aftercare.<sup>2</sup></p>
<p>That said, every person and situation is unique, and effective treatment is customized to the individual. Thus, it’s important to work with addiction and healthcare professionals to determine which <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/levels-of-care" target="_blank" rel="noopener">level of care</a> is right for you or your loved one.</p>
<p>Generally speaking, professionals often recommend IOPs for people with mild to moderate addiction who don’t need 24/7 supervision or detox.<sup>1</sup> These are usually people who need a more structured treatment than traditional outpatient programs but also need to take care of responsibilities at home.</p>
<p>Patients well suited to IOPs also have sober living conditions, transportation to and from therapy, and continued motivation to complete the program.<sup>3</sup> And although support from friends and family isn’t mandatory for a successful recovery, it certainly helps, particularly with an outpatient program.<sup>4 </sup>Compared to inpatient programs where patients receive care 24/7 and are surrounded by supportive peers and professionals, an unsupportive home environment can be problematic for recovery.</p>
<div class="portal_video" id="portal_video-69a79faf13863"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"type":"wistia","id":"g3l9k1w5p4","title":"iOP for FL Residents"}}</script></div>
<h2>Where Can You Find an IOP in Florida?</h2>
<p>IOPs throughout Florida tend to be located near metropolitan areas such as Miami, Jacksonville, Fort Lauderdale, Orlando, and Tampa. However, treatment centers of all types are scattered throughout the state. For example, the state boasts 547 substance use treatment centers, of which 216 offer IOPs.<sup>5</sup></p>
<p>You may also be able to participate in Florida IOPs and other types of treatment through <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth/florida" target="_blank" rel="noopener">telehealth</a>, which enlists phone and online communication technologies to connect patients to doctors, therapists, addiction specialists, and peers. Depending on the program, telehealth may allow you to participate in the same services offered in an in-person setting—such as individual therapy, group therapy, and medication-assisted treatment (MAT)—from the comfort of your home.<sup>6 </sup></p>
<p>According to <a href="https://www.FindTreatment.gov" target="_blank" rel="noopener">FindTreatment.gov</a>, which is offered by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), 368 treatment facilities in Florida offer telehealth rehab services, and 147 of the state’s 216 IOP providers have telehealth options.<sup>5</sup></p>
<p><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/florida" target="_blank" rel="noopener">If you’re looking for treatment centers in Florida</a>, you’re in luck. Two world-class  facilities are in your backyard. An industry leader in addiction care, American Addiction Centers (AAC) offers several facilities scattered throughout the United States, including <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/recovery-first-hollywood" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Recovery First Treatment Center in Hollywood, FL</a>, and <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/river-oaks" target="_blank" rel="noopener">River Oaks Treatment Center in Riverview, FL</a>.</p>
<p>If an IOP isn’t a great fit for your needs, chances are the facility has other options to serve you. For example, both Recovery First Treatment Center and River Oaks Treatment Center provide options such as:<sup>4</sup></p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Medical detox</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Inpatient or residential rehab.</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Outpatient.</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs).</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/partial-hospitalization-programs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Partial hospitalization programs (PHPs).</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/aftercare" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Aftercare.</a></li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Telehealth</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>Reach out to one of our admissions navigators at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a79faf1387d"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span> to learn more about these facilities and to explore the various levels of care available.</p>
<h2><strong>What Substances Do IOPs in Florida Treat?</strong></h2>
<p>Intensive outpatient programs in Florida can typically treat a variety of substance addictions. For example, the IOPs at <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/recovery-first-hollywood" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Recovery First Treatment Center</a> and <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/river-oaks" target="_blank" rel="noopener">River Oaks Treatment Center</a> treat disorders related to the following substances and more:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Alcohol</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/benzodiazepine/symptoms-and-signs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Benzodiazepines</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/cocaine/signs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cocaine</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/fentanyl" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Fentanyl</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/hallucinogens" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Hallucinogens</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/heroin/rehab-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Heroin</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Opioids</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/meth/rehab-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Methamphetamine</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/prescription-drugs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Prescription drugs</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>Beyond drug and alcohol addiction services, 175 IOPs in Florida provide treatment for <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders" target="_blank" rel="noopener">co-occurring mental conditions</a>, such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders.<sup>5,7 </sup> Treating both substance use and mental health disorders at the same time is generally more effective than treating these conditions independently.<sup>4</sup></p>
<h2><strong>How Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in Florida Cost?</strong></h2>
<p>The cost of IOP programs in Florida vary based on several factors, including:</p>
<ul>
<li>Services required.</li>
<li>Length of treatment.</li>
<li>Facility fees and whether the treatment center is in-network.</li>
<li>Insurance coverage.</li>
</ul>
<p>For those with insurance, your level of insurance coverage (including deductibles, co-pays, etc.) can affect how much you pay out-of-pocket for treatment. The best way to determine exactly how much you’ll pay is to verify your insurance benefits. Reach out to AAC at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a79faf1388d"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span>, and an admissions navigator can assist you with this process.</p>
<p>Keep in mind, a lack of insurance doesn’t have to be a barrier to getting the care you need. There are other ways to pay for IOPs as described below.</p>
<h2><strong>Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Florida?</strong></h2>
<p>Insurance typically provides full or partial coverage for IOPs in Florida thanks to a pair of laws that protect your access to mental health and addiction services. The <a href="https://www.cms.gov/CCIIO/Programs-and-Initiatives/Other-Insurance-Protections/mhpaea_factsheet" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA)</a> ensures equal addiction and substance use disorder benefits.<sup>8 </sup>And the <a href="https://www.hhs.gov/answers/health-insurance-reform/does-the-aca-cover-individuals-with-mental-health-problems/index.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="broken_link">Affordable Care Act (ACA)</a> guarantees coverage for essential health benefits, including substance use treatment.<sup>9 </sup>Thus, insurance companies must cover substance use treatment to some extent, and that coverage can’t have special limitations compared to other benefits.<sup>8,9  </sup>Still, your level of coverage depends on your insurance policy and whether the treatment you choose is seen as medically necessary.</p>
<div class="c-callout c-callout--borders--all">
		
		
<h3>Medicare, Medicaid, and Tricare</h3>
<p>Government and military insurance programs may also pay for full or partial IOP treatment based on your eligibility. Of the 216 Florida intensive outpatient programs, the following number of IOPs accept these respective insurance types:<sup>5</sup> <sup> </sup></p>
<ul>
<li>45: <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/medicare" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Medicare</a>.</li>
<li>79: <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/using-medicaid-to-pay-for-rehab" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Medicaid</a>.</li>
<li>88: <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/tricare" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Tricare</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>

	</p></div>
<p><strong>But what if you don’t have insurance?</strong></p>
<p>Other payment options are avilable, including:<sup>6,10 </sup></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Payment plans and sliding fee scales.</strong> Sliding fee scales and/or financing options are available through 30 IOPs in Florida.</li>
<li><strong>Grants and scholarships.</strong> Several local, state, and government organizations offer grants and scholarships for those seeking addiction treatment. For example, the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) provides block grants for those struggling with addiction. Currently 27 IOPs in Florida accept block grant funding.</li>
<li><strong>Indian Health Service (IHS)/Tribal/Urban—ITU Funds. </strong>Considered part of the Indian healthcare system, ITU funds are accepted by 31 IOPs in Florida.</li>
<li><strong>Loans/Private Pay.</strong> It’s also possible to pay for addiction treatment with cash or credit, and you may be able to secure a loan from a friend, relative, or company specializing in addiction treatment lending.</li>
</ul>
<p>Keep in mind, however, that you may be eligible for <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free" target="_blank" rel="noopener">free</a> and/or <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/state-funded" target="_blank" rel="noopener">state-funded programs</a> that are available based on income, special population status, and more.</p>
<p>Whether you’re looking for an IOP, telehealth services, outpatient care, detox, or even sober living, <a href="/treatment-centers" target="_blank" rel="noopener">American Addiction Centers</a> can help. AAC admissions navigators at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a79faf13924"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span> can not only help you explore treatment options but also verify your insurance and support you in taking the critical first steps toward recovery.</p>

</script><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"_sentryTraceData":"867aa31535174b23a65727d7243cb451-a7b8028a6579d760-0","_sentryBaggage":"sentry-environment=production,sentry-release=odsUSnXHa7Xks8o0SB39f,sentry-public_key=96a2ce264cf34a8eb5bbd664e8a614e8,sentry-trace_id=867aa31535174b23a65727d7243cb451,sentry-sample_rate=0.01,sentry-transaction=%2F%5B...pathname%5D,sentry-sampled=false","data":{"id":101876,"date":"2023-06-06T16:25:38","modified":"2024-12-09T18:02:53","slug":"florida","type":"page","link":"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/florida","title":{"rendered":"Intensive Outpatient Programs in Florida: Finding IOP Programs in Florida"},"content":{"rendered":"\u003cdiv\u003e\u003ch2\u003eWhat is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIn Florida and elsewhere, intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) are a form of outpatient treatment for substance use disorders. Provided in clinics, rehab facilities, counseling offices, and more, IOPs in Florida offer therapies similar to that of inpatient facilities. However, this care is provided at a slightly lower intensity, and patients return to their homes at night—rather than staying in the facility 24/7.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eIntensive outpatient programs\u003c/a\u003e, which are more rigorous than traditional \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eoutpatient treatment\u003c/a\u003e, can be entry points to treatment (potentially following detox) for some patients. For others, IOPs are a step down from \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003einpatient programs\u003c/a\u003e, as they help patients to transition successfully from inpatient rehab into the new normal of everyday life.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eWhile IOPs vary in terms of levels of intensity and participation requirements, many IOP programs in Florida require 6 to 30 hours per week of treatment and last for 90 days.\u003csup\u003e1,2 \u003c/sup\u003eOften these programs are followed by traditional outpatient care and/or aftercare.\u003csup\u003e2\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThat said, every person and situation is unique, and effective treatment is customized to the individual. Thus, it’s important to work with addiction and healthcare professionals to determine which \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/levels-of-care\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003elevel of care\u003c/a\u003e is right for you or your loved one.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eGenerally speaking, professionals often recommend IOPs for people with mild to moderate addiction who don’t need 24/7 supervision or detox.\u003csup\u003e1\u003c/sup\u003e These are usually people who need a more structured treatment than traditional outpatient programs but also need to take care of responsibilities at home.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003ePatients well suited to IOPs also have sober living conditions, transportation to and from therapy, and continued motivation to complete the program.\u003csup\u003e3\u003c/sup\u003e And although support from friends and family isn’t mandatory for a successful recovery, it certainly helps, particularly with an outpatient program.\u003csup\u003e4 \u003c/sup\u003eCompared to inpatient programs where patients receive care 24/7 and are surrounded by supportive peers and professionals, an unsupportive home environment can be problematic for recovery.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_video\" id=\"portal_video-69a79faf13863\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"type\":\"wistia\",\"id\":\"g3l9k1w5p4\",\"title\":\"iOP for FL Residents\"}}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003eWhere Can You Find an IOP in Florida?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIOPs throughout Florida tend to be located near metropolitan areas such as Miami, Jacksonville, Fort Lauderdale, Orlando, and Tampa. However, treatment centers of all types are scattered throughout the state. For example, the state boasts 547 substance use treatment centers, of which 216 offer IOPs.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eYou may also be able to participate in Florida IOPs and other types of treatment through \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth/florida\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003etelehealth\u003c/a\u003e, which enlists phone and online communication technologies to connect patients to doctors, therapists, addiction specialists, and peers. Depending on the program, telehealth may allow you to participate in the same services offered in an in-person setting—such as individual therapy, group therapy, and medication-assisted treatment (MAT)—from the comfort of your home.\u003csup\u003e6 \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAccording to \u003ca href=\"http://www.FindTreatment.gov\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eFindTreatment.gov\u003c/a\u003e, which is offered by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), 368 treatment facilities in Florida offer telehealth rehab services, and 147 of the state’s 216 IOP providers have telehealth options.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/florida\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eIf you’re looking for treatment centers in Florida\u003c/a\u003e, you’re in luck. Two world-class  facilities are in your backyard. An industry leader in addiction care, American Addiction Centers (AAC) offers several facilities scattered throughout the United States, including \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/recovery-first-hollywood\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eRecovery First Treatment Center in Hollywood, FL\u003c/a\u003e, and \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/river-oaks\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eRiver Oaks Treatment Center in Riverview, FL\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIf an IOP isn’t a great fit for your needs, chances are the facility has other options to serve you. For example, both Recovery First Treatment Center and River Oaks Treatment Center provide options such as:\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMedical detox\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eInpatient or residential rehab.\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eOutpatient.\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eIntensive outpatient programs (IOPs).\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/partial-hospitalization-programs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003ePartial hospitalization programs (PHPs).\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/aftercare\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAftercare.\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eTelehealth\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eReach out to one of our admissions navigators at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a79faf1387d\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e to learn more about these facilities and to explore the various levels of care available.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat Substances Do IOPs in Florida Treat?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIntensive outpatient programs in Florida can typically treat a variety of substance addictions. For example, the IOPs at \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/recovery-first-hollywood\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eRecovery First Treatment Center\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/river-oaks\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eRiver Oaks Treatment Center\u003c/a\u003e treat disorders related to the following substances and more:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAlcohol\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/benzodiazepine/symptoms-and-signs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eBenzodiazepines\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/cocaine/signs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eCocaine\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/fentanyl\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eHallucinogens\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/heroin/rehab-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eOpioids\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/meth/rehab-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003ePrescription drugs\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eBeyond drug and alcohol addiction services, 175 IOPs in Florida provide treatment for \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eco-occurring mental conditions\u003c/a\u003e, such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders.\u003csup\u003e5,7 \u003c/sup\u003e Treating both substance use and mental health disorders at the same time is generally more effective than treating these conditions independently.\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHow Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in Florida Cost?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThe cost of IOP programs in Florida vary based on several factors, including:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eServices required.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLength of treatment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFacility fees and whether the treatment center is in-network.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInsurance coverage.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eFor those with insurance, your level of insurance coverage (including deductibles, co-pays, etc.) can affect how much you pay out-of-pocket for treatment. The best way to determine exactly how much you’ll pay is to verify your insurance benefits. Reach out to AAC at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a79faf1388d\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e, and an admissions navigator can assist you with this process.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eKeep in mind, a lack of insurance doesn’t have to be a barrier to getting the care you need. There are other ways to pay for IOPs as described below.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDoes Insurance Cover IOPs in Florida?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eInsurance typically provides full or partial coverage for IOPs in Florida thanks to a pair of laws that protect your access to mental health and addiction services. The \u003ca href=\"https://www.cms.gov/CCIIO/Programs-and-Initiatives/Other-Insurance-Protections/mhpaea_factsheet\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA)\u003c/a\u003e ensures equal addiction and substance use disorder benefits.\u003csup\u003e8 \u003c/sup\u003eAnd the \u003ca href=\"https://www.hhs.gov/answers/health-insurance-reform/does-the-aca-cover-individuals-with-mental-health-problems/index.html\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" class=\"broken_link\"\u003eAffordable Care Act (ACA)\u003c/a\u003e guarantees coverage for essential health benefits, including substance use treatment.\u003csup\u003e9 \u003c/sup\u003eThus, insurance companies must cover substance use treatment to some extent, and that coverage can’t have special limitations compared to other benefits.\u003csup\u003e8,9  \u003c/sup\u003eStill, your level of coverage depends on your insurance policy and whether the treatment you choose is seen as medically necessary.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"c-callout c-callout--borders--all\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\t\t\n\u003ch3\u003eMedicare, Medicaid, and Tricare\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eGovernment and military insurance programs may also pay for full or partial IOP treatment based on your eligibility. Of the 216 Florida intensive outpatient programs, the following number of IOPs accept these respective insurance types:\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e \u003csup\u003e \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e45: \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/medicare\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMedicare\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e79: \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/using-medicaid-to-pay-for-rehab\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMedicaid\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e88: \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/tricare\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eTricare\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBut what if you don’t have insurance?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eOther payment options are avilable, including:\u003csup\u003e6,10 \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePayment plans and sliding fee scales.\u003c/strong\u003e Sliding fee scales and/or financing options are available through 30 IOPs in Florida.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGrants and scholarships.\u003c/strong\u003e Several local, state, and government organizations offer grants and scholarships for those seeking addiction treatment. For example, the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) provides block grants for those struggling with addiction. Currently 27 IOPs in Florida accept block grant funding.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIndian Health Service (IHS)/Tribal/Urban—ITU Funds. \u003c/strong\u003eConsidered part of the Indian healthcare system, ITU funds are accepted by 31 IOPs in Florida.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLoans/Private Pay.\u003c/strong\u003e It’s also possible to pay for addiction treatment with cash or credit, and you may be able to secure a loan from a friend, relative, or company specializing in addiction treatment lending.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eKeep in mind, however, that you may be eligible for \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003efree\u003c/a\u003e and/or \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/state-funded\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003estate-funded programs\u003c/a\u003e that are available based on income, special population status, and more.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eWhether you’re looking for an IOP, telehealth services, outpatient care, detox, or even sober living, \u003ca href=\"/treatment-centers\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAmerican Addiction Centers\u003c/a\u003e can help. AAC admissions navigators at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a79faf13924\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e can not only help you explore treatment options but also verify your insurance and support you in taking the critical first steps toward recovery.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_sources\" id=\"portal_sources-69a79faf13a2f\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003col\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSubstance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration. Intensive Outpatient Programs in Florida: Finding IOP Programs in Florida

 Written by: Mariah Bourne 

 Edited by: Linda Armstrong 

Updated Dec 9, 2024

 3 min read · 5 sections 

 
 
 
 Medical Detox 
 Insurance Accepted 
 Same Day Admission 

Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Florida offer treatment for substance use disorders in an outpatient setting, allowing patients to live in their own homes during rehab. However, therapies at Florida IOPs are similar to inpatient facilities; they’re just provided at a slightly lower intensity. Learn more about Florida IOPs including typical treatment durations, level of care provided, and payment options.

What you will learn:

 
 
IOP basics (e.g., level of care, length of treatment, etc.).

 
 
How to locate a Florida IOP.

 
 
Insurance coverage and payment options. 

What is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?

In Florida and elsewhere, intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) are a form of outpatient treatment for substance use disorders. Provided in clinics, rehab facilities, counseling offices, and more, IOPs in Florida offer therapies similar to that of inpatient facilities. However, this care is provided at a slightly lower intensity, and patients return to their homes at night—rather than staying in the facility 24/7.

 Intensive outpatient programs , which are more rigorous than traditional outpatient treatment , can be entry points to treatment (potentially following detox) for some patients. For others, IOPs are a step down from inpatient programs , as they help patients to transition successfully from inpatient rehab into the new normal of everyday life.

While IOPs vary in terms of levels of intensity and participation requirements, many IOP programs in Florida require 6 to 30 hours per week of treatment and last for 90 days. 1,2 Often these programs are followed by traditional outpatient care and/or aftercare. 2 

That said, every person and situation is unique, and effective treatment is customized to the individual. Thus, it’s important to work with addiction and healthcare professionals to determine which level of care is right for you or your loved one.

Generally speaking, professionals often recommend IOPs for people with mild to moderate addiction who don’t need 24/7 supervision or detox. 1 These are usually people who need a more structured treatment than traditional outpatient programs but also need to take care of responsibilities at home.

Patients well suited to IOPs also have sober living conditions, transportation to and from therapy, and continued motivation to complete the program. 3 And although support from friends and family isn’t mandatory for a successful recovery, it certainly helps, particularly with an outpatient program. 4 Compared to inpatient programs where patients receive care 24/7 and are surrounded by supportive peers and professionals, an unsupportive home environment can be problematic for recovery.

 

Where Can You Find an IOP in Florida?

IOPs throughout Florida tend to be located near metropolitan areas such as Miami, Jacksonville, Fort Lauderdale, Orlando, and Tampa. However, treatment centers of all types are scattered throughout the state. For example, the state boasts 547 substance use treatment centers, of which 216 offer IOPs. 5 

You may also be able to participate in Florida IOPs and other types of treatment through telehealth , which enlists phone and online communication technologies to connect patients to doctors, therapists, addiction specialists, and peers. Depending on the program, telehealth may allow you to participate in the same services offered in an in-person setting—such as individual therapy, group therapy, and medication-assisted treatment (MAT)—from the comfort of your home. 6 

According to FindTreatment.gov , which is offered by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), 368 treatment facilities in Florida offer telehealth rehab services, and 147 of the state’s 216 IOP providers have telehealth options. 5 

 If you’re looking for treatment centers in Florida , you’re in luck. Two world-class  facilities are in your backyard. An industry leader in addiction care, American Addiction Centers (AAC) offers several facilities scattered throughout the United States, including Recovery First Treatment Center in Hollywood, FL , and River Oaks Treatment Center in Riverview, FL .

If an IOP isn’t a great fit for your needs, chances are the facility has other options to serve you. For example, both Recovery First Treatment Center and River Oaks Treatment Center provide options such as: 4 

 

 Medical detox .

 Inpatient or residential rehab. 

 Outpatient. 

 Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs). 

 Partial hospitalization programs (PHPs). 

 Aftercare. 

 Telehealth .

 

Reach out to one of our admissions navigators at to learn more about these facilities and to explore the various levels of care available.

 What Substances Do IOPs in Florida Treat? 

Intensive outpatient programs in Florida can typically treat a variety of substance addictions. For example, the IOPs at Recovery First Treatment Center and River Oaks Treatment Center treat disorders related to the following substances and more:

 

 Alcohol .

 Benzodiazepines .

 Cocaine .

 Fentanyl .

 Hallucinogens .

 Heroin .

 Opioids .

 Methamphetamine .

 Prescription drugs .

 

Beyond drug and alcohol addiction services, 175 IOPs in Florida provide treatment for co-occurring mental conditions , such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders. 5,7  Treating both substance use and mental health disorders at the same time is generally more effective than treating these conditions independently. 4 

 How Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in Florida Cost? 

The cost of IOP programs in Florida vary based on several factors, including:

 

Services required.

Length of treatment.

Facility fees and whether the treatment center is in-network.

Insurance coverage.

 

For those with insurance, your level of insurance coverage (including deductibles, co-pays, etc.) can affect how much you pay out-of-pocket for treatment. The best way to determine exactly how much you’ll pay is to verify your insurance benefits. Reach out to AAC at , and an admissions navigator can assist you with this process.

Keep in mind, a lack of insurance doesn’t have to be a barrier to getting the care you need. There are other ways to pay for IOPs as described below.

 Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Florida? 

Insurance typically provides full or partial coverage for IOPs in Florida thanks to a pair of laws that protect your access to mental health and addiction services. The Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA) ensures equal addiction and substance use disorder benefits. 8 And the Affordable Care Act (ACA) guarantees coverage for essential health benefits, including substance use treatment. 9 Thus, insurance companies must cover substance use treatment to some extent, and that coverage can’t have special limitations compared to other benefits. 8,9   Still, your level of coverage depends on your insurance policy and whether the treatment you choose is seen as medically necessary.

 
 

Medicare, Medicaid, and Tricare

Government and military insurance programs may also pay for full or partial IOP treatment based on your eligibility. Of the 216 Florida intensive outpatient programs, the following number of IOPs accept these respective insurance types: 5   

 

45: Medicare .

79: Medicaid .

88: Tricare .

 

 

 But what if you don’t have insurance? 

Other payment options are avilable, including: 6,10 

 

 Payment plans and sliding fee scales. Sliding fee scales and/or financing options are available through 30 IOPs in Florida.

 Grants and scholarships. Several local, state, and government organizations offer grants and scholarships for those seeking addiction treatment. For example, the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) provides block grants for those struggling with addiction. Currently 27 IOPs in Florida accept block grant funding.

 Indian Health Service (IHS)/Tribal/Urban—ITU Funds. Considered part of the Indian healthcare system, ITU funds are accepted by 31 IOPs in Florida.

 Loans/Private Pay. It’s also possible to pay for addiction treatment with cash or credit, and you may be able to secure a loan from a friend, relative, or company specializing in addiction treatment lending.

 

Keep in mind, however, that you may be eligible for free and/or state-funded programs that are available based on income, special population status, and more.

Whether you’re looking for an IOP, telehealth services, outpatient care, detox, or even sober living, American Addiction Centers can help. AAC admissions navigators at can not only help you explore treatment options but also verify your insurance and support you in taking the critical first steps toward recovery.
