		position: fixed;
		z-index: 1031;
		top: 0;
		left: 0;

		width: 100%;
		height: 2px;
	}

	/* Fancy blur effect */
	#nprogress .peg {
		display: block;
		position: absolute;
		right: 0px;
		width: 100px;
		height: 100%;
		box-shadow: 0 0 10px #337fbd, 0 0 5px #337fbd;
		opacity: 1;

		-webkit-transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
		-ms-transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
		transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
	}

	/* Remove these to get rid of the spinner */
	#nprogress .spinner {
		display: block;
		position: fixed;
		z-index: 1031;
		top: 15px;
		right: 15px;
	}

	#nprogress .spinner-icon {
		width: 18px;
		height: 18px;
		box-sizing: border-box;

		border: solid 2px transparent;
		border-top-color: #29d;
		border-left-color: #29d;
		border-radius: 50%;

		-webkit-animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
		animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent {
		overflow: hidden;
		position: relative;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .spinner,
	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .bar {
		position: absolute;
	}
	.bcFloat,
	.widget-floating__teaser {
		display: none !important;
	}
	@-webkit-keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}
	@keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}

	/* font_awesome styles */
	.fa,.fab,.fal,.far,.fas {
		-moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
		-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
		display: inline-block;
		font-style: normal;
		font-variant: normal;
		text-rendering: auto;
		line-height: 1
	}

options.</div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 container"><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 content"><div id="wordpress-content" class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555 toc__container"><div class="sc-ae1ffb5e-0 emMzuR"><div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div><h2><strong>What Is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?</strong></h2>
<p>Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Nevada and elsewhere are a type of outpatient treatment for substance use disorders. With an <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost" target="_blank" rel="noopener">IOP</a>, patients attend treatment sessions at a care facility but then return to their own homes at night. IOPs provide a higher level of care than traditional <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">outpatient treatment</a>, but they’re a step down from <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient" target="_blank" rel="noopener">inpatient programs</a> where patients stay in a rehab facility 24/7.<sup>1,2 </sup>That said, IOPs provide similar care and therapies to those found in inpatient settings, just at a lower intensity.</p>
<p>Intensive outpatient programs can help people transition out of inpatient treatment and into their new normal. Or, IOPs can be the start of a patient’s recovery journey, perhaps following a short <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox" target="_blank" rel="noopener">detox</a> period.</p>
<p>Provided in clinics, rehab facilities, counseling offices, and more, IOPs in Nevada usually:<sup>1</sup></p>
<ul>
<li>Last for 90 days.</li>
<li>Require patients to attend 6 to 30 hours a week.</li>
<li>Are followed by traditional outpatient continuing care.</li>
</ul>
<p>Since there’s no one-size-fits all solution to treatment, it’s important to work with addiction and healthcare professionals to determine which level of treatment is right for you or your loved one. However, professionals typically recommend intensive outpatient programs for people with mild to moderate addiction who don’t need 24/7 care.<sup>4</sup></p>
<p>While not required, positive at-home support systems can be helpful for IOP patients.<sup>5 </sup>In an inpatient program, patients receive care and attention 24/7, and they’re surrounded by counselors, medical professionals, and peers. If patients’ home environments lack the support of family and friends, this environment can sometimes hinder progress and undermine success in an outpatient program. Additionally, outpatient care is best suited to those with transportation to attend sessions, living arrangements that support sober living, and the motivation to attend consistently.<sup>6</sup></p>
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74df9f1828"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<h3>ASAM Levels of Care<\/h3>\n<p>To provide some context for the various types of rehab, the American Society of Addiction Medicine (ASAM) has identified the following five <a href=\"https:\/\/americanaddictioncenters.org\/rehab-guide\/levels-of-care\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">levels of care<\/a>, which include various sublevels. IOPs are a 2.1 on ASAM\u2019s continuum of care.<sup>3<\/sup><\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>.5 Prevention\/Early Intervention.<\/li>\n<li>1 Outpatient Services.<\/li>\n<li>2 Intensive Outpatient Services \/ Partial Hospitalization Services.\n<ul>\n<li><strong>2.1 Intensive Outpatient Services.<\/strong><\/li>\n<li>2.5 <a href=\"https:\/\/americanaddictioncenters.org\/rehab-guide\/partial-hospitalization-programs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Partial Hospitalization Programs<\/a>.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<\/li>\n<li>3 Residential\/Inpatient Services.<\/li>\n<li>4 Medically Managed Intensive Inpatient Services.<br \/>\n"}</script></div>

<h2><strong>Where Can You Find an IOP in Nevada?</strong></h2>
<p><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/nevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Treatment centers in Nevada</a> tend to be centered around the state’s two major metropolitan areas: Reno-Carson City-Fernley and Las Vegas-Henderson-Paradise. However, FindTreatment.gov, which is offered by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), indicates that Nevada has 70 substance use treatment facilities scattered throughout the state, ranging from locales such as Owyhee near the Idaho border to Ely in in the far eastern part of the state. Among these 70 Nevada facilities, 39 provide intensive outpatient programs.<sup>7</sup></p>
<p>Additionally, a variety of healthcare services—including addiction treatment, mental health services, primary care, specialist consultations, and chronic disease management—are offered via telehealth.<sup>8</sup> Roughly 120 treatment facilities in Nevada offer telehealth options.<sup>7</sup></p>
<p>If you live in Nevada, a renowned addiction treatment facility is in your backyard. American Addiction Centers (AAC) is a well-known, industry leader in addiction care and has treatment facilities across the country, including the Desert Hope Treatment Center in Las Vegas, which offers both IOP and <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth/nevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener">telehealth</a> addiction treatment.</p>
<h2><strong>What Substances Do IOPs in Nevada Treat?</strong></h2>
<p>Many Nevada IOPs treat a variety of addictions. For example, the IOP at <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/desert-hope/outpatient-las-vegas" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Desert Hope Outpatient</a> in Las Vegas treats disorders related to the following substances and more:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Alcohol</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/benzodiazepine/symptoms-and-signs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Benzodiazepines</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/cocaine/signs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cocaine</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/fentanyl" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Fentanyl</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/hallucinogens" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Hallucinogens</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/heroin/rehab-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Heroin</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Opioids</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/meth/rehab-treatment" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Methamphetamine</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/prescription-drugs" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Prescription drugs</a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>Additionally, some IOPs—including 32 in Nevada—also offer treatment for <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders" target="_blank" rel="noopener">co-occurring mental conditions</a>, such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders.<sup>7,9</sup> This dual treatment is important as it may positively impact recovery. According to the National Institute on Drug Abuse, treating both mental health conditions and substance use disorders at the same time is more effective than treating them separately.<sup>9</sup></p>
<h2><strong>How Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in Nevada Cost?</strong></h2>
<p>The cost of an intensive outpatient program in Nevada can vary depending on several factors, such as insurance coverage (including deductibles, co-pays, etc.), whether the facility is in-network or out of network with a provider, facility fees, length of stay, desired amenities, and more.</p>
<p>For those with insurance, the best way to understand your costs is to verify your insurance. Contact American Addiction Centers at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a74df9f1843"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span>, and one of the admissions navigators can do the legwork for you.</p>
<p>If you don’t have insurance, there are other ways to pay for IOPs. Some IOPs—including 12 in Nevada—provide sliding fee scales based on your income and/or financing options.<sup>7 </sup>Plus, various state and federal organizations offer <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free" target="_blank" rel="noopener">free rehab</a> as well as <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/state-funded" target="_blank" rel="noopener">state-funded programs</a> that may meet your needs.</p>
<h2><strong> Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Nevada?</strong></h2>
<p>Thanks to the Affordable Care Act (ACA) and the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA), treatment for substance use disorders is at least partly covered by insurance.<sup>10,11</sup> In effect, these regulations stipulate that insurance providers must offer equal coverage for mental and physical health conditions.</p>
<p>Medicare, Medicaid, and Tricare (a health program for uniformed service members, retirees, and their families) also offer full or partial coverage for treatment such as IOPs.</p>
<h2>Intensive Outpatient Program in Las Vegas, Nevada</h2>
<p>If you or your loved one is ready to take your first step toward recovery, American Addiction Centers can help. Whether you’re looking for an intensive outpatient program in Las Vegas, Nevada or another form of treatment, AAC admissions navigators at <span class="portal_phone_link" id="portal_phone_link-69a74df9f184e"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"helpline":"true"},"children":""}</script></span> can not only help you explore AAC’s <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers" target="_blank" rel="noopener">treatment centers</a> but also <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/verify-insurance" target="_blank" rel="noopener">verify your insurance</a> and answer your questions about detox, inpatient, outpatient, sober living, telehealth services, and more.</p>

</script><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"_sentryTraceData":"07c9721683bd4eec9eaff698d25568d1-a8685ce62a5c50eb-0","_sentryBaggage":"sentry-environment=production,sentry-release=odsUSnXHa7Xks8o0SB39f,sentry-public_key=96a2ce264cf34a8eb5bbd664e8a614e8,sentry-trace_id=07c9721683bd4eec9eaff698d25568d1,sentry-sample_rate=0.01,sentry-transaction=%2F%5B...pathname%5D,sentry-sampled=false","data":{"id":101860,"date":"2023-06-06T10:52:29","modified":"2025-03-21T18:32:11","slug":"nevada","type":"page","link":"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/nevada","title":{"rendered":"Intensive Outpatient Treatment Programs in Nevada: Finding an IOP in Nevada"},"content":{"rendered":"\u003cdiv\u003e\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat Is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIntensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Nevada and elsewhere are a type of outpatient treatment for substance use disorders. With an \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eIOP\u003c/a\u003e, patients attend treatment sessions at a care facility but then return to their own homes at night. IOPs provide a higher level of care than traditional \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eoutpatient treatment\u003c/a\u003e, but they’re a step down from \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003einpatient programs\u003c/a\u003e where patients stay in a rehab facility 24/7.\u003csup\u003e1,2 \u003c/sup\u003eThat said, IOPs provide similar care and therapies to those found in inpatient settings, just at a lower intensity.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIntensive outpatient programs can help people transition out of inpatient treatment and into their new normal. Or, IOPs can be the start of a patient’s recovery journey, perhaps following a short \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003edetox\u003c/a\u003e period.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eProvided in clinics, rehab facilities, counseling offices, and more, IOPs in Nevada usually:\u003csup\u003e1\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eLast for 90 days.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eRequire patients to attend 6 to 30 hours a week.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAre followed by traditional outpatient continuing care.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eSince there’s no one-size-fits all solution to treatment, it’s important to work with addiction and healthcare professionals to determine which level of treatment is right for you or your loved one. However, professionals typically recommend intensive outpatient programs for people with mild to moderate addiction who don’t need 24/7 care.\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eWhile not required, positive at-home support systems can be helpful for IOP patients.\u003csup\u003e5 \u003c/sup\u003eIn an inpatient program, patients receive care and attention 24/7, and they’re surrounded by counselors, medical professionals, and peers. If patients’ home environments lack the support of family and friends, this environment can sometimes hinder progress and undermine success in an outpatient program. Additionally, outpatient care is best suited to those with transportation to attend sessions, living arrangements that support sober living, and the motivation to attend consistently.\u003csup\u003e6\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74df9f1828\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003ch3\u003eASAM Levels of Care\u003c\\/h3\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003eTo provide some context for the various types of rehab, the American Society of Addiction Medicine (ASAM) has identified the following five \u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/levels-of-care\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener\\\"\u003elevels of care\u003c\\/a\u003e, which include various sublevels. IOPs are a 2.1 on ASAM\\u2019s continuum of care.\u003csup\u003e3\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e.5 Prevention\\/Early Intervention.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e1 Outpatient Services.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e2 Intensive Outpatient Services \\/ Partial Hospitalization Services.\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e2.1 Intensive Outpatient Services.\u003c\\/strong\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e2.5 \u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/partial-hospitalization-programs\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener\\\"\u003ePartial Hospitalization Programs\u003c\\/a\u003e.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e3 Residential\\/Inpatient Services.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e4 Medically Managed Intensive Inpatient Services.\u003cbr \\/\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhere Can You Find an IOP in Nevada?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/nevada\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eTreatment centers in Nevada\u003c/a\u003e tend to be centered around the state’s two major metropolitan areas: Reno-Carson City-Fernley and Las Vegas-Henderson-Paradise. However, FindTreatment.gov, which is offered by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), indicates that Nevada has 70 substance use treatment facilities scattered throughout the state, ranging from locales such as Owyhee near the Idaho border to Ely in in the far eastern part of the state. Among these 70 Nevada facilities, 39 provide intensive outpatient programs.\u003csup\u003e7\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAdditionally, a variety of healthcare services—including addiction treatment, mental health services, primary care, specialist consultations, and chronic disease management—are offered via telehealth.\u003csup\u003e8\u003c/sup\u003e Roughly 120 treatment facilities in Nevada offer telehealth options.\u003csup\u003e7\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIf you live in Nevada, a renowned addiction treatment facility is in your backyard. American Addiction Centers (AAC) is a well-known, industry leader in addiction care and has treatment facilities across the country, including the Desert Hope Treatment Center in Las Vegas, which offers both IOP and \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/telehealth/nevada\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003etelehealth\u003c/a\u003e addiction treatment.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat Substances Do IOPs in Nevada Treat?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eMany Nevada IOPs treat a variety of addictions. For example, the IOP at \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers/desert-hope/outpatient-las-vegas\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eDesert Hope Outpatient\u003c/a\u003e in Las Vegas treats disorders related to the following substances and more:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eAlcohol\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/benzodiazepine/symptoms-and-signs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eBenzodiazepines\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/cocaine/signs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eCocaine\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/fentanyl\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eFentanyl\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/hallucinogens\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eHallucinogens\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids/heroin/rehab-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eHeroin\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/opioids\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eOpioids\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/stimulants/meth/rehab-treatment\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eMethamphetamine\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/prescription-drugs\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003ePrescription drugs\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAdditionally, some IOPs—including 32 in Nevada—also offer treatment for \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003eco-occurring mental conditions\u003c/a\u003e, such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders.\u003csup\u003e7,9\u003c/sup\u003e This dual treatment is important as it may positively impact recovery. According to the National Institute on Drug Abuse, treating both mental health conditions and substance use disorders at the same time is more effective than treating them separately.\u003csup\u003e9\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHow Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in Nevada Cost?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThe cost of an intensive outpatient program in Nevada can vary depending on several factors, such as insurance coverage (including deductibles, co-pays, etc.), whether the facility is in-network or out of network with a provider, facility fees, length of stay, desired amenities, and more.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eFor those with insurance, the best way to understand your costs is to verify your insurance. Contact American Addiction Centers at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a74df9f1843\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e, and one of the admissions navigators can do the legwork for you.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIf you don’t have insurance, there are other ways to pay for IOPs. Some IOPs—including 12 in Nevada—provide sliding fee scales based on your income and/or financing options.\u003csup\u003e7 \u003c/sup\u003ePlus, various state and federal organizations offer \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003efree rehab\u003c/a\u003e as well as \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/state-funded\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003estate-funded programs\u003c/a\u003e that may meet your needs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Nevada?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThanks to the Affordable Care Act (ACA) and the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA), treatment for substance use disorders is at least partly covered by insurance.\u003csup\u003e10,11\u003c/sup\u003e In effect, these regulations stipulate that insurance providers must offer equal coverage for mental and physical health conditions.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eMedicare, Medicaid, and Tricare (a health program for uniformed service members, retirees, and their families) also offer full or partial coverage for treatment such as IOPs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003eIntensive Outpatient Program in Las Vegas, Nevada\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eIf you or your loved one is ready to take your first step toward recovery, American Addiction Centers can help. Whether you’re looking for an intensive outpatient program in Las Vegas, Nevada or another form of treatment, AAC admissions navigators at \u003cspan class=\"portal_phone_link\" id=\"portal_phone_link-69a74df9f184e\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"helpline\":\"true\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/span\u003e can not only help you explore AAC’s \u003ca href=\"http://americanaddictioncenters.org/treatment-centers\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003etreatment centers\u003c/a\u003e but also \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/verify-insurance\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"\u003everify your insurance\u003c/a\u003e and answer your questions about detox, inpatient, outpatient, sober living, telehealth services, and more.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_sources\" id=\"portal_sources-69a74df9f1975\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003col\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eCenter for Substance Abuse Treatment. \u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/store.samhsa.gov\\/sites\\/default\\/files\\/SAMHSA_Digital_Download\\/sma13-4182.pdf\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener\\\"\u003eSubstance abuse: Clinical issues in intensive outpatient treatment. Intensive Outpatient Treatment Programs in Nevada: Finding an IOP in Nevada

 Written by: Sherilyn Moore, B.A. 

 Edited by: Linda Armstrong 

Updated Mar 21, 2025

 3 min read · 6 sections 

 
 
 
 Medical Detox 
 Insurance Accepted 
 Same Day Admission 

Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Nevada offer similar care to inpatient facilities but at a lower intensity. Plus, as a type of outpatient care, IOPs allow patients to live in their own homes during treatment. Learn more about Nevada IOPs including the typical duration of stay, the level of care, and how to pay for treatment.

What you will learn:

 
 
IOP insights (e.g., duration, benefits, level of care).

 
 
Where and how to find an IOP in Nevada.

 
 
Types of disorders treated.

 
 
Costs, insurance coverage, and payment options.

 What Is an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)? 

Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) in Nevada and elsewhere are a type of outpatient treatment for substance use disorders. With an IOP , patients attend treatment sessions at a care facility but then return to their own homes at night. IOPs provide a higher level of care than traditional outpatient treatment , but they’re a step down from inpatient programs where patients stay in a rehab facility 24/7. 1,2 That said, IOPs provide similar care and therapies to those found in inpatient settings, just at a lower intensity.

Intensive outpatient programs can help people transition out of inpatient treatment and into their new normal. Or, IOPs can be the start of a patient’s recovery journey, perhaps following a short detox period.

Provided in clinics, rehab facilities, counseling offices, and more, IOPs in Nevada usually: 1 

 

Last for 90 days.

Require patients to attend 6 to 30 hours a week.

Are followed by traditional outpatient continuing care.

 

Since there’s no one-size-fits all solution to treatment, it’s important to work with addiction and healthcare professionals to determine which level of treatment is right for you or your loved one. However, professionals typically recommend intensive outpatient programs for people with mild to moderate addiction who don’t need 24/7 care. 4 

While not required, positive at-home support systems can be helpful for IOP patients. 5 In an inpatient program, patients receive care and attention 24/7, and they’re surrounded by counselors, medical professionals, and peers. If patients’ home environments lack the support of family and friends, this environment can sometimes hinder progress and undermine success in an outpatient program. Additionally, outpatient care is best suited to those with transportation to attend sessions, living arrangements that support sober living, and the motivation to attend consistently. 6 

 

 Where Can You Find an IOP in Nevada? 

 Treatment centers in Nevada tend to be centered around the state’s two major metropolitan areas: Reno-Carson City-Fernley and Las Vegas-Henderson-Paradise. However, FindTreatment.gov, which is offered by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), indicates that Nevada has 70 substance use treatment facilities scattered throughout the state, ranging from locales such as Owyhee near the Idaho border to Ely in in the far eastern part of the state. Among these 70 Nevada facilities, 39 provide intensive outpatient programs. 7 

Additionally, a variety of healthcare services—including addiction treatment, mental health services, primary care, specialist consultations, and chronic disease management—are offered via telehealth. 8 Roughly 120 treatment facilities in Nevada offer telehealth options. 7 

If you live in Nevada, a renowned addiction treatment facility is in your backyard. American Addiction Centers (AAC) is a well-known, industry leader in addiction care and has treatment facilities across the country, including the Desert Hope Treatment Center in Las Vegas, which offers both IOP and telehealth addiction treatment.

 What Substances Do IOPs in Nevada Treat? 

Many Nevada IOPs treat a variety of addictions. For example, the IOP at Desert Hope Outpatient in Las Vegas treats disorders related to the following substances and more:

 

 Alcohol .

 Benzodiazepines .

 Cocaine .

 Fentanyl .

 Hallucinogens .

 Heroin .

 Opioids .

 Methamphetamine .

 Prescription drugs .

 

Additionally, some IOPs—including 32 in Nevada—also offer treatment for co-occurring mental conditions , such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, schizophrenia, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and personality disorders. 7,9 This dual treatment is important as it may positively impact recovery. According to the National Institute on Drug Abuse, treating both mental health conditions and substance use disorders at the same time is more effective than treating them separately. 9 

 How Much Does an Intensive Outpatient Program in Nevada Cost? 

The cost of an intensive outpatient program in Nevada can vary depending on several factors, such as insurance coverage (including deductibles, co-pays, etc.), whether the facility is in-network or out of network with a provider, facility fees, length of stay, desired amenities, and more.

For those with insurance, the best way to understand your costs is to verify your insurance. Contact American Addiction Centers at , and one of the admissions navigators can do the legwork for you.

If you don’t have insurance, there are other ways to pay for IOPs. Some IOPs—including 12 in Nevada—provide sliding fee scales based on your income and/or financing options. 7 Plus, various state and federal organizations offer free rehab as well as state-funded programs that may meet your needs.

 Does Insurance Cover IOPs in Nevada? 

Thanks to the Affordable Care Act (ACA) and the Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA), treatment for substance use disorders is at least partly covered by insurance. 10,11 In effect, these regulations stipulate that insurance providers must offer equal coverage for mental and physical health conditions.

Medicare, Medicaid, and Tricare (a health program for uniformed service members, retirees, and their families) also offer full or partial coverage for treatment such as IOPs.

Intensive Outpatient Program in Las Vegas, Nevada

If you or your loved one is ready to take your first step toward recovery, American Addiction Centers can help. Whether you’re looking for an intensive outpatient program in Las Vegas, Nevada or another form of treatment, AAC admissions navigators at can not only help you explore AAC’s treatment centers but also verify your insurance and answer your questions about detox, inpatient, outpatient, sober living, telehealth services, and more.

		border: solid 2px transparent;
		border-top-color: #29d;
		border-left-color: #29d;
		border-radius: 50%;

		-webkit-animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
		animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent {
		overflow: hidden;
		position: relative;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .spinner,
	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .bar {
		position: absolute;
	}
	.bcFloat,
	.widget-floating__teaser {
		display: none !important;
	}
	@-webkit-keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			-webkit-transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}
	@keyframes nprogress-spinner {
		0% {
			transform: rotate(0deg);
		}
		100% {
			transform: rotate(360deg);
		}
	}

<!-- -->Levels of Care</button></div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-4001556473 hero__summary">What are partial hospitalization programs (PHP)? What type of therapies are offered, and what levels of care do PHPs provide? Who may be well-suited for this type of treatment, and how much does it cost? Explore the answers to these questions and more, and learn how to find a partial hospitalization program near you.
</div><div class="jsx-4001556473 hero__highlights"><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f container"><div style="margin:0 0 15px 0" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f text-sm bold">What you will learn:</div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">What a partial hospitalization program (PHP) entails.</div></div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">Which patients may be well-suited for PHPs.</div></div><div class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f item"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" style="transform:rotate(0deg)"><path d="M20.8389 6.69461L8.81811 18.7154L3.16125 13.0586L4.57125 11.6486L8.81811 15.8854L19.4289 5.28461L20.8389 6.69461Z" fill="var(--success)"></path></svg><div style="margin-left:15px" class="jsx-acda82bbe253fc7f">PHP costs and payment options.</div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 container"><div class="jsx-123217fb97058cc4 jsx-726436161 content"><div id="wordpress-content" class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555 toc__container"><div class="sc-ae1ffb5e-0 emMzuR"><div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div><h2><b>What is a Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)?</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Partial hospitalization programs (PHP)—aka day treatment—are an intensive and highly structured form of <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment">outpatient rehab</a> that allows patients to live at home as opposed to within an</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">inpatient treatment center</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">According to the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), treatment programs featuring daily treatment for 6 hours per day or more constitute PHPs. That said, treatment duration and requirements vary by facility.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">1</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">For example, PHPs offered by American Addiction Centers facilities typically involve treatment for 3 to 7 days per week in 6- to 8-hour time blocks.</span></p>
<h2><b>How PHPs Fit Into the Addiction Treatment Continuum</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">To understand how a PHP compares to other types of treatment, it helps to look at the</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/levels-of-care"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">levels of care</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> set forth by the American Society of Addiction Medicine (ASAM). ASAM developed a widely used set of guidelines to determine the placement, transfer, and discharge of people seeking addiction and</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">co-occurring disorder</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> treatment.</span></p>
<p><b>PHP care is lower in intensity than inpatient and residential treatment—but higher than:</b></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Traditional </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment"><span style="font-weight: 400;">outpatient care</span></a></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs), another structured outpatient treatment option</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">6</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">This middle-ground intensity typically comes at a somewhat lower cost than inpatient care, while still offering access to clinical therapies and medical oversight.</span></p>
<h2><b>When are Partial Hospitalization Programs Appropriate?</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Partial hospitalization programs are often employed following inpatient treatment as a form of continuing care or as a first step in recovery, possibly immediately after detox.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">PHPs are appropriate for patients that:</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">6</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Don’t require (or have completed) detox.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Don’t need 24-hour monitoring to ensure safety and abstinence.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Have a sober home environment that supports—rather than challenges—their recovery. (When home environments are challenging, </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/sober-living"><span style="font-weight: 400;">sober living</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> or </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/sober-living/oxford-house"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Oxford house</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> environments may serve as alternative living environments during PHP treatment.)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Can secure reliable transportation to and from treatment.</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Usually, PHP patients have either completed or don’t require </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox"><span style="font-weight: 400;">detox</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">, and they don’t need 24-hour supervision to ensure their safety and foster ongoing sobriety.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">6  </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">These individuals may still be early in recovery but have reached a point where they can engage in structured treatment during the day while returning home to sleep. Treatment providers might refer someone to a PHP if they’re medically stable, motivated to participate in intensive therapy, and able to manage their own basic daily responsibilities. </span></p>
<p><b>What Services Are Included in a PHP?</b></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Representing the midway point between</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">inpatient</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> and</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">traditional outpatient</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> treatment, PHPs offer many of the same therapeutic interventions found in inpatient treatment.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">3</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> These include:</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">4,5</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Medication management.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individual and group therapy.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Family therapy.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Educational groups.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Occupational and recreational therapies.</span></li>
</ul>
<div class="c-callout c-callout--borders--all">
		
		
<h2><b>PHP Treatment Objectives for Drug and Alcohol Addiction</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Partial hospitalization programs (PHPs) provide a host of benefits, including psychosocial support and individual treatment, both of which can help patients develop coping and relapse-prevention management strategies.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">6</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> While each PHP is unique, SAMHSA has established several goals forPHPs, including:</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">1</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Achieving abstinence</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Fostering behavior changes that support a drug- and alcohol-free lifestyle.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Improving coping and problem-solving skills</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Facilitating participation in community-based support systems such as 12-step programs</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Assisting patients in identifying and dealing with psychosocial issues (e.g., employment, housing, adherence to probation limitations)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Aiding patients in developing a positive social network</span></li>
</ul>
<p>

	</p></div>
<h2><b>What Is the Difference between PHPs and IOPs?</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Both PHPs and IOPs are forms of outpatient </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">care</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> Note that an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) is a structured addiction treatment option that provides ongoing support through regular therapy sessions while allowing individuals to live at home and maintain daily responsibilities. </span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Here are some key similarities and differences highlighted in the table below:</span></p>
<table>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><b>Category</b></td>
<td><b>Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)</b></td>
<td><b>Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)</b></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><b>Intensity</b></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">One step down from the intensity of residential care</span>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Services mirror residential programs with the added flexibility of living at home</span></p></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Less intensive than PHP, but more intensive than traditional outpatient therapy</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><b>Weekly Time Commitment</b></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">6 hours </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">on each participating day, most often 5 days a week</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Requires a minimum of 9 hours of weekly programming, often in 3 sessions lasting 3 hours each</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">1</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><b>Treatment Du</b><b>ration</b></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">At least 3 months</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">2 months to 1 year</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><b>Flexibility</b></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">More structured and time-intensive; less flexibility for those with outside responsibilities</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">IOPs offer a bit more flexibility with regard to treatment requirements</span>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Some IOPs offer evening or virtual options to suit different schedules</span></p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><b>Best Suited For</b></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individuals who need intensive clinical support but not 24/7 supervision</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Those whose personal and professional responsibilities make it impossible to participate in a PHP</span>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Those who don’t need the intensive support of a PHP, perhaps due to:</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Already completing a higher level of care </span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Having  a strong support system at home and  doing well in their recovery</span></li>
</ul>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2><b>What Happens During PHP for Drug or Alcohol Addiction?</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Many of the therapies and services provided in </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient"><span style="font-weight: 400;">inpatient care</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> are also available in partial hospitalization programs. While individual programs vary, typical services provided by PHPs include:</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">1</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Personalized treatment planning and reviews.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individual and group counseling and therapy.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Psychoeducational programming.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Medication management and pharmacotherapy.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Case management.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">24-hour crisis coverage.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Monitoring for drug and alcohol use.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Medical treatment as needed.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Psychiatric exams and psychotherapy.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Community-based support groups.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Family involvement and counseling.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Vocational training and employment services.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Biopsychosocial screening and assessments.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Transition management and discharge planning.</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Addiction specialists throughout various levels of care use a wide range of behavioral therapies. These typically include </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/therapy-treatment/cognitive-behavioral-therapy"><span style="font-weight: 400;">cognitive-behavioral therapy</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> (CBT), which addresses factors contributing to drug and</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">alcohol misuse</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> and builds relapse-prevention skills by helping patients to learn, identify, and change behaviors.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">7 </span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Other therapies, such as motivational interviewing (MI) and contingency management (CM), are particularly helpful </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">early in treatment, </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">as they encourage patients to engage in recovery and build a plan for change.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">7</span></p>
<h2><b>What to Look for in a Partial Hospitalization Program for Drug or Alcohol Addiction</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you’ve determined that a PHP is a viable solution for you or a loved one, it’s important to shop around for a quality rehab facility that offers PHPs. Factors to consider during your search include:</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Location. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Since you won’t be living at the facility, it’s important that the PHP you choose is either relatively close to your home or near other suitable housing, such as outpatient living environments, sober living facilities, Oxford houses, etc.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Facility accreditations. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Reputable rehabs are accredited by various organizations, including both state and national certification programs. Look for accreditations and certifications such as:</span>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><a href="https://www.carf.org/"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF)</span></a></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><a href="https://www.jointcommission.org/" class="broken_link"><span style="font-weight: 400;">The Joint Commission</span></a></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">HIPAA Seal of Compliance</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><a href="https://www.nabh.org/we-are-the-national-association-for-behavioral-healthcare"><span style="font-weight: 400;">National Association for Behavioral Healthcare</span></a></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><a href="https://www.bbb.org/"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Better Business Bureau Rankings</span></a></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Staff licenses/certifications</b><span style="font-weight: 400;">. High-quality PHPs typically include a mix of specialized professionals as opposed to a couple of generalized individuals that wear multiple hats. Most of these individuals should have state or national licenses such as:</span>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">LADC (Licensed Alcohol and Drug Counselor)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">LPC (Licensed Professional Counselor)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">CAC (Certified Addictions Counselor)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">CCDP (Certified Co-occurring Disorders Counselor)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">NCAC (National Certified Addiction Counselor)</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="2"><span style="font-weight: 400;">MAC (Master Addiction Counselor)</span></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Reviews. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Explore online reviews to see what patients are saying about the facility and the PHP. Also, inquire with the facility to see if it has an alumni program, which can offer valuable support following PHP treatment and throughout recovery.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Payment options. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you have insurance, determine if the facility offering the PHP is in-network with your provider for the PHP level of care and how much you’ll pay out of pocket for treatment. If you don’t have insurance, ask about other payment options, such as sliding-fee scales, payment plans, and more, as described below.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Special-population programs. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Some PHPs cater to specific populations, such as </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/veterans/rehab-resources"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Veterans</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">, single-parent households, pregnant women, trauma survivors, members of the LGBTQ+ community, and more. Members of these groups may feel more comfortable and supported when surrounded by their peers.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Therapies. </b>Some people prefer specific therapies over other options. If you have strong preferences, inquire about the therapies available through each program.</li>
</ul>
<h2><b>How Much Does a PHP for Drug or Alcohol Addiction Cost?</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">PHPs are a cost-effective treatment option compared to inpatient or residential treatment programs.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">3</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> That said, costs vary widely depending on each patient’s needs and factors such as:</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">4</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Individual facility pricing.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Length of treatment.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Community versus hospital-based programs.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Insurance coverage.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Facility location.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Payment type (e.g., out of pocket, sliding-fee scale, financing, health insurance, etc.).</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you have insurance, PHP coverage varies by plan. The best way to understand your costs is to verify your benefits with your carrier.  Or, contact American Addiction Centers at (629) 302-1607 to find out </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/verify-insurance"><span style="font-weight: 400;">what your plan may cover</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> at one of our facilities.</span></p>
<h3><b>How to Pay For PHP Without Insurance Coverage</b></h3>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">For those without insurance, there are</span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/payment-options"> <span style="font-weight: 400;">other ways to pay for PHP treatment</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;">, such as:</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Cash, credit, and loans. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Most facilities accept payment in the form of cash or credit. Additionally, many patients secure funding via loans from family, friends, and/or institutions specializing in addiction financing.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Sliding-fee scales and payment plans</b><span style="font-weight: 400;">. Some PHPs will adjust the cost of treatment for qualified individuals based on their incomes, or they may offer payment plans that allow you to pay for treatment over time.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Low cost/free rehabs.</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> Various counties and states offer </span><a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free"><span style="font-weight: 400;">low-cost or free treatment</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> for those with special circumstances or limited income.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Local, state, or federal funding.</b><span style="font-weight: 400;"> Many organizations such as SAMHSA provide grants and scholarships for treatment.</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">With facilities scattered across the country, American Addiction Centers (AAC) offers a host of PHP payment options.</span></p>
<h2><b>How to Find a Partial Hospitalization Program Near You</b></h2>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The U.S. is home to more than 1,900 PHPs, including several facilities under the American Addiction Centers umbrella.</span><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Contact American Addiction Centers at </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">(629) 302-1607</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"> to find a PHP near you. Our admissions navigators are available 24/7 to answer your confidential treatment questions, verify your insurance, explore payment options, and help you take your first steps toward recovery today.</span></p>
