</script><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"_sentryTraceData":"13ada316b751420398e53eba2494afe5-9086f784c47e93bb-0","_sentryBaggage":"sentry-environment=production,sentry-release=odsUSnXHa7Xks8o0SB39f,sentry-public_key=96a2ce264cf34a8eb5bbd664e8a614e8,sentry-trace_id=13ada316b751420398e53eba2494afe5,sentry-sample_rate=0.01,sentry-transaction=%2F%5B...pathname%5D,sentry-sampled=false","data":{"id":104050,"date":"2023-08-11T09:12:40","modified":"2026-01-27T14:51:05","slug":"partial-hospitalization-programs","type":"page","link":"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment/partial-hospitalization-programs","title":{"rendered":"Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) for Addiction"},"content":{"rendered":"\u003cdiv\u003e\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eWhat is a Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePartial hospitalization programs (PHP)—aka day treatment—are an intensive and highly structured form of \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\"\u003eoutpatient rehab\u003c/a\u003e that allows patients to live at home as opposed to within an\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003einpatient treatment center\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAccording to the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), treatment programs featuring daily treatment for 6 hours per day or more constitute PHPs. That said, treatment duration and requirements vary by facility.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e1\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFor example, PHPs offered by American Addiction Centers facilities typically involve treatment for 3 to 7 days per week in 6- to 8-hour time blocks.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eHow PHPs Fit Into the Addiction Treatment Continuum\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eTo understand how a PHP compares to other types of treatment, it helps to look at the\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/levels-of-care\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003elevels of care\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e set forth by the American Society of Addiction Medicine (ASAM). ASAM developed a widely used set of guidelines to determine the placement, transfer, and discharge of people seeking addiction and\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/co-occurring-disorders\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eco-occurring disorder\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e treatment.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cb\u003ePHP care is lower in intensity than inpatient and residential treatment—but higher than:\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eTraditional \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eoutpatient care\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIntensive outpatient programs (IOPs), another structured outpatient treatment option\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e6\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eThis middle-ground intensity typically comes at a somewhat lower cost than inpatient care, while still offering access to clinical therapies and medical oversight.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eWhen are Partial Hospitalization Programs Appropriate?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePartial hospitalization programs are often employed following inpatient treatment as a form of continuing care or as a first step in recovery, possibly immediately after detox.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePHPs are appropriate for patients that:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e6\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eDon’t require (or have completed) detox.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eDon’t need 24-hour monitoring to ensure safety and abstinence.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eHave a sober home environment that supports—rather than challenges—their recovery. (When home environments are challenging, \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/insurance-coverage/sober-living\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003esober living\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e or \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/sober-living/oxford-house\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eOxford house\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e environments may serve as alternative living environments during PHP treatment.)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCan secure reliable transportation to and from treatment.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eUsually, PHP patients have either completed or don’t require \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/drug-detox\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003edetox\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e, and they don’t need 24-hour supervision to ensure their safety and foster ongoing sobriety.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e6  \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eThese individuals may still be early in recovery but have reached a point where they can engage in structured treatment during the day while returning home to sleep. Treatment providers might refer someone to a PHP if they’re medically stable, motivated to participate in intensive therapy, and able to manage their own basic daily responsibilities. \u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cb\u003eWhat Services Are Included in a PHP?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eRepresenting the midway point between\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003einpatient\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e and\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/outpatient-treatment\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003etraditional outpatient\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e treatment, PHPs offer many of the same therapeutic interventions found in inpatient treatment.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e3\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e These include:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e4,5\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMedication management.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIndividual and group therapy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFamily therapy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eEducational groups.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eOccupational and recreational therapies.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"c-callout c-callout--borders--all\"\u003e\n\t\t\n\t\t\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003ePHP Treatment Objectives for Drug and Alcohol Addiction\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePartial hospitalization programs (PHPs) provide a host of benefits, including psychosocial support and individual treatment, both of which can help patients develop coping and relapse-prevention management strategies.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e6\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e While each PHP is unique, SAMHSA has established several goals forPHPs, including:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e1\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAchieving abstinence\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFostering behavior changes that support a drug- and alcohol-free lifestyle.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eImproving coping and problem-solving skills\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFacilitating participation in community-based support systems such as 12-step programs\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAssisting patients in identifying and dealing with psychosocial issues (e.g., employment, housing, adherence to probation limitations)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAiding patients in developing a positive social network\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\n\n\t\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eWhat Is the Difference between PHPs and IOPs?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eBoth PHPs and IOPs are forms of outpatient \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ecare\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e Note that an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) is a structured addiction treatment option that provides ongoing support through regular therapy sessions while allowing individuals to live at home and maintain daily responsibilities. \u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eHere are some key similarities and differences highlighted in the table below:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eCategory\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003ePartial Hospitalization Program (PHP)\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eIntensive Outpatient Program (IOP)\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eIntensity\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eOne step down from the intensity of residential care\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eServices mirror residential programs with the added flexibility of living at home\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eLess intensive than PHP, but more intensive than traditional outpatient therapy\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eWeekly Time Commitment\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e6 hours \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eon each participating day, most often 5 days a week\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eRequires a minimum of 9 hours of weekly programming, often in 3 sessions lasting 3 hours each\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e1\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eTreatment Du\u003c/b\u003e\u003cb\u003eration\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAt least 3 months\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e8\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e2 months to 1 year\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e8\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eFlexibility\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMore structured and time-intensive; less flexibility for those with outside responsibilities\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIOPs offer a bit more flexibility with regard to treatment requirements\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eSome IOPs offer evening or virtual options to suit different schedules\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cb\u003eBest Suited For\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIndividuals who need intensive clinical support but not 24/7 supervision\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e8\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eThose whose personal and professional responsibilities make it impossible to participate in a PHP\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eThose who don’t need the intensive support of a PHP, perhaps due to:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAlready completing a higher level of care \u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eHaving  a strong support system at home and  doing well in their recovery\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eWhat Happens During PHP for Drug or Alcohol Addiction?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMany of the therapies and services provided in \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/inpatient\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003einpatient care\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e are also available in partial hospitalization programs. While individual programs vary, typical services provided by PHPs include:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e1\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePersonalized treatment planning and reviews.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIndividual and group counseling and therapy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePsychoeducational programming.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMedication management and pharmacotherapy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCase management.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e24-hour crisis coverage.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMonitoring for drug and alcohol use.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMedical treatment as needed.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePsychiatric exams and psychotherapy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCommunity-based support groups.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFamily involvement and counseling.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eVocational training and employment services.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eBiopsychosocial screening and assessments.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eTransition management and discharge planning.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eAddiction specialists throughout various levels of care use a wide range of behavioral therapies. These typically include \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/therapy-treatment/cognitive-behavioral-therapy\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ecognitive-behavioral therapy\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e (CBT), which addresses factors contributing to drug and\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/alcohol\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ealcohol misuse\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e and builds relapse-prevention skills by helping patients to learn, identify, and change behaviors.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e7 \u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eOther therapies, such as motivational interviewing (MI) and contingency management (CM), are particularly helpful \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eearly in treatment, \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eas they encourage patients to engage in recovery and build a plan for change.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e7\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eWhat to Look for in a Partial Hospitalization Program for Drug or Alcohol Addiction\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIf you’ve determined that a PHP is a viable solution for you or a loved one, it’s important to shop around for a quality rehab facility that offers PHPs. Factors to consider during your search include:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eLocation. \u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eSince you won’t be living at the facility, it’s important that the PHP you choose is either relatively close to your home or near other suitable housing, such as outpatient living environments, sober living facilities, Oxford houses, etc.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eFacility accreditations. \u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eReputable rehabs are accredited by various organizations, including both state and national certification programs. Look for accreditations and certifications such as:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://www.carf.org/\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCommission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://www.jointcommission.org/\" class=\"broken_link\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eThe Joint Commission\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eHIPAA Seal of Compliance\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://www.nabh.org/we-are-the-national-association-for-behavioral-healthcare\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eNational Association for Behavioral Healthcare\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://www.bbb.org/\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eBetter Business Bureau Rankings\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eStaff licenses/certifications\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e. High-quality PHPs typically include a mix of specialized professionals as opposed to a couple of generalized individuals that wear multiple hats. Most of these individuals should have state or national licenses such as:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eLADC (Licensed Alcohol and Drug Counselor)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eLPC (Licensed Professional Counselor)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCAC (Certified Addictions Counselor)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCCDP (Certified Co-occurring Disorders Counselor)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eNCAC (National Certified Addiction Counselor)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMAC (Master Addiction Counselor)\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eReviews. \u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eExplore online reviews to see what patients are saying about the facility and the PHP. Also, inquire with the facility to see if it has an alumni program, which can offer valuable support following PHP treatment and throughout recovery.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003ePayment options. \u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIf you have insurance, determine if the facility offering the PHP is in-network with your provider for the PHP level of care and how much you’ll pay out of pocket for treatment. If you don’t have insurance, ask about other payment options, such as sliding-fee scales, payment plans, and more, as described below.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eSpecial-population programs. \u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eSome PHPs cater to specific populations, such as \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/veterans/rehab-resources\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eVeterans\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e, single-parent households, pregnant women, trauma survivors, members of the LGBTQ+ community, and more. Members of these groups may feel more comfortable and supported when surrounded by their peers.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eTherapies. \u003c/b\u003eSome people prefer specific therapies over other options. If you have strong preferences, inquire about the therapies available through each program.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eHow Much Does a PHP for Drug or Alcohol Addiction Cost?\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePHPs are a cost-effective treatment option compared to inpatient or residential treatment programs.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e3\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e That said, costs vary widely depending on each patient’s needs and factors such as:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e4\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIndividual facility pricing.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eLength of treatment.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eCommunity versus hospital-based programs.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eInsurance coverage.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFacility location.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ePayment type (e.g., out of pocket, sliding-fee scale, financing, health insurance, etc.).\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIf you have insurance, PHP coverage varies by plan. The best way to understand your costs is to verify your benefits with your carrier.  Or, contact American Addiction Centers at (629) 302-1607 to find out \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/verify-insurance\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003ewhat your plan may cover\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e at one of our facilities.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch3\u003e\u003cb\u003eHow to Pay For PHP Without Insurance Coverage\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eFor those without insurance, there are\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/payment-options\"\u003e \u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eother ways to pay for PHP treatment\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e, such as:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eCash, credit, and loans. \u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eMost facilities accept payment in the form of cash or credit. Additionally, many patients secure funding via loans from family, friends, and/or institutions specializing in addiction financing.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eSliding-fee scales and payment plans\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e. Some PHPs will adjust the cost of treatment for qualified individuals based on their incomes, or they may offer payment plans that allow you to pay for treatment over time.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eLow cost/free rehabs.\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e Various counties and states offer \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003elow-cost or free treatment\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e for those with special circumstances or limited income.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli style=\"font-weight: 400;\" aria-level=\"1\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eLocal, state, or federal funding.\u003c/b\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e Many organizations such as SAMHSA provide grants and scholarships for treatment.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eWith facilities scattered across the country, American Addiction Centers (AAC) offers a host of PHP payment options.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003e\u003cb\u003eHow to Find a Partial Hospitalization Program Near You\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eThe U.S. is home to more than 1,900 PHPs, including several facilities under the American Addiction Centers umbrella.\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e8\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e \u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eContact American Addiction Centers at \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e(629) 302-1607\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e to find a PHP near you. Our admissions navigators are available 24/7 to answer your confidential treatment questions, verify your insurance, explore payment options, and help you take your first steps toward recovery today.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_sources\" id=\"portal_sources-69a752a281767\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003col\u003e\\n\u003cli style=\\\"font-weight: 400;\\\" aria-level=\\\"1\\\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\\\"font-weight: 400;\\\"\u003eSubstance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration. Partial hospitalization programs (PHP)—aka day treatment—are an intensive and highly structured form of outpatient rehab that allows patients to live at home as opposed to within an inpatient treatment center. Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) for Addiction

 Written by: Mariah Bourne 

 Edited by: Linda Armstrong 

Updated Jan 27, 2026

 4 min read · 9 sections 

 
 
 
 Medical Detox 
 Insurance Accepted 
 Same Day Admission 
 Levels of Care 

What are partial hospitalization programs (PHP)? What type of therapies are offered, and what levels of care do PHPs provide? Who may be well-suited for this type of treatment, and how much does it cost? Explore the answers to these questions and more, and learn how to find a partial hospitalization program near you.

What you will learn:

 
 
What a partial hospitalization program (PHP) entails.

 
 
Which patients may be well-suited for PHPs.

 
 
PHP costs and payment options.

 What is a Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)? 

 Partial hospitalization programs (PHP)—aka day treatment—are an intensive and highly structured form of outpatient rehab that allows patients to live at home as opposed to within an inpatient treatment center . 

 According to the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), treatment programs featuring daily treatment for 6 hours per day or more constitute PHPs. That said, treatment duration and requirements vary by facility. 1 

 For example, PHPs offered by American Addiction Centers facilities typically involve treatment for 3 to 7 days per week in 6- to 8-hour time blocks. 

 How PHPs Fit Into the Addiction Treatment Continuum 

 To understand how a PHP compares to other types of treatment, it helps to look at the levels of care set forth by the American Society of Addiction Medicine (ASAM). ASAM developed a widely used set of guidelines to determine the placement, transfer, and discharge of people seeking addiction and co-occurring disorder treatment. 

 PHP care is lower in intensity than inpatient and residential treatment—but higher than: 

 

 Traditional outpatient care 

 Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs), another structured outpatient treatment option 6 

 

 This middle-ground intensity typically comes at a somewhat lower cost than inpatient care, while still offering access to clinical therapies and medical oversight. 

 When are Partial Hospitalization Programs Appropriate? 

 Partial hospitalization programs are often employed following inpatient treatment as a form of continuing care or as a first step in recovery, possibly immediately after detox. 

 PHPs are appropriate for patients that: 6 

 

 Don’t require (or have completed) detox. 

 Don’t need 24-hour monitoring to ensure safety and abstinence. 

 Have a sober home environment that supports—rather than challenges—their recovery. (When home environments are challenging, sober living or Oxford house environments may serve as alternative living environments during PHP treatment.) 

 Can secure reliable transportation to and from treatment. 

 

 Usually, PHP patients have either completed or don’t require detox , and they don’t need 24-hour supervision to ensure their safety and foster ongoing sobriety. 6  These individuals may still be early in recovery but have reached a point where they can engage in structured treatment during the day while returning home to sleep. Treatment providers might refer someone to a PHP if they’re medically stable, motivated to participate in intensive therapy, and able to manage their own basic daily responsibilities.  

 What Services Are Included in a PHP? 

 Representing the midway point between inpatient and traditional outpatient treatment, PHPs offer many of the same therapeutic interventions found in inpatient treatment. 3 These include: 4,5 

 

 Medication management. 

 Individual and group therapy. 

 Family therapy. 

 Educational groups. 

 Occupational and recreational therapies. 

 

 
 

 PHP Treatment Objectives for Drug and Alcohol Addiction 

 Partial hospitalization programs (PHPs) provide a host of benefits, including psychosocial support and individual treatment, both of which can help patients develop coping and relapse-prevention management strategies. 6 While each PHP is unique, SAMHSA has established several goals forPHPs, including: 1 

 

 Achieving abstinence 

 Fostering behavior changes that support a drug- and alcohol-free lifestyle. 

 Improving coping and problem-solving skills 

 Facilitating participation in community-based support systems such as 12-step programs 

 Assisting patients in identifying and dealing with psychosocial issues (e.g., employment, housing, adherence to probation limitations) 

 Aiding patients in developing a positive social network 

 

 

 What Is the Difference between PHPs and IOPs? 

 Both PHPs and IOPs are forms of outpatient care . Note that an Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) is a structured addiction treatment option that provides ongoing support through regular therapy sessions while allowing individuals to live at home and maintain daily responsibilities.  

 Here are some key similarities and differences highlighted in the table below: 

 
 
 
 Category 
 Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) 
 Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) 
 
 
 Intensity 
 One step down from the intensity of residential care 

 Services mirror residential programs with the added flexibility of living at home 
 
 Less intensive than PHP, but more intensive than traditional outpatient therapy 
 
 
 Weekly Time Commitment 
 6 hours on each participating day, most often 5 days a week 
 Requires a minimum of 9 hours of weekly programming, often in 3 sessions lasting 3 hours each 1 
 
 
 Treatment Du ration 
 At least 3 months 8 
 2 months to 1 year 8 
 
 
 Flexibility 
 More structured and time-intensive; less flexibility for those with outside responsibilities 
 IOPs offer a bit more flexibility with regard to treatment requirements 

 Some IOPs offer evening or virtual options to suit different schedules 
 
 
 
 Best Suited For 
 Individuals who need intensive clinical support but not 24/7 supervision 8 
 Those whose personal and professional responsibilities make it impossible to participate in a PHP 

 Those who don’t need the intensive support of a PHP, perhaps due to: 

 

 Already completing a higher level of care  

 Having  a strong support system at home and  doing well in their recovery 

 
 
 
 
 

 What Happens During PHP for Drug or Alcohol Addiction? 

 Many of the therapies and services provided in inpatient care are also available in partial hospitalization programs. While individual programs vary, typical services provided by PHPs include: 1 

 

 Personalized treatment planning and reviews. 

 Individual and group counseling and therapy. 

 Psychoeducational programming. 

 Medication management and pharmacotherapy. 

 Case management. 

 24-hour crisis coverage. 

 Monitoring for drug and alcohol use. 

 Medical treatment as needed. 

 Psychiatric exams and psychotherapy. 

 Community-based support groups. 

 Family involvement and counseling. 

 Vocational training and employment services. 

 Biopsychosocial screening and assessments. 

 Transition management and discharge planning. 

 

 Addiction specialists throughout various levels of care use a wide range of behavioral therapies. These typically include cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), which addresses factors contributing to drug and alcohol misuse and builds relapse-prevention skills by helping patients to learn, identify, and change behaviors. 7  

 Other therapies, such as motivational interviewing (MI) and contingency management (CM), are particularly helpful early in treatment, as they encourage patients to engage in recovery and build a plan for change. 7 

 What to Look for in a Partial Hospitalization Program for Drug or Alcohol Addiction 

 If you’ve determined that a PHP is a viable solution for you or a loved one, it’s important to shop around for a quality rehab facility that offers PHPs. Factors to consider during your search include: 

 

 Location. Since you won’t be living at the facility, it’s important that the PHP you choose is either relatively close to your home or near other suitable housing, such as outpatient living environments, sober living facilities, Oxford houses, etc. 

 Facility accreditations. Reputable rehabs are accredited by various organizations, including both state and national certification programs. Look for accreditations and certifications such as: 
 

 Commission on Accreditation of Rehabilitation Facilities (CARF) 

 The Joint Commission 

 HIPAA Seal of Compliance 

 National Association for Behavioral Healthcare 

 Better Business Bureau Rankings 

 

 Staff licenses/certifications . High-quality PHPs typically include a mix of specialized professionals as opposed to a couple of generalized individuals that wear multiple hats. Most of these individuals should have state or national licenses such as: 
 

 LADC (Licensed Alcohol and Drug Counselor) 

 LPC (Licensed Professional Counselor) 

 CAC (Certified Addictions Counselor) 

 CCDP (Certified Co-occurring Disorders Counselor) 

 NCAC (National Certified Addiction Counselor) 

 MAC (Master Addiction Counselor) 

 

 Reviews. Explore online reviews to see what patients are saying about the facility and the PHP. Also, inquire with the facility to see if it has an alumni program, which can offer valuable support following PHP treatment and throughout recovery. 

 Payment options. If you have insurance, determine if the facility offering the PHP is in-network with your provider for the PHP level of care and how much you’ll pay out of pocket for treatment. If you don’t have insurance, ask about other payment options, such as sliding-fee scales, payment plans, and more, as described below. 

 Special-population programs. Some PHPs cater to specific populations, such as Veterans , single-parent households, pregnant women, trauma survivors, members of the LGBTQ+ community, and more. Members of these groups may feel more comfortable and supported when surrounded by their peers. 

 Therapies. Some people prefer specific therapies over other options. If you have strong preferences, inquire about the therapies available through each program.

 

 How Much Does a PHP for Drug or Alcohol Addiction Cost? 

 PHPs are a cost-effective treatment option compared to inpatient or residential treatment programs. 3 That said, costs vary widely depending on each patient’s needs and factors such as: 4 

 

 Individual facility pricing. 

 Length of treatment. 

 Community versus hospital-based programs. 

 Insurance coverage. 

 Facility location. 

 Payment type (e.g., out of pocket, sliding-fee scale, financing, health insurance, etc.). 

 

 If you have insurance, PHP coverage varies by plan. The best way to understand your costs is to verify your benefits with your carrier.  Or, contact American Addiction Centers at (629) 302-1607 to find out what your plan may cover at one of our facilities. 

 How to Pay For PHP Without Insurance Coverage 

 For those without insurance, there are other ways to pay for PHP treatment , such as: 

 

 Cash, credit, and loans. Most facilities accept payment in the form of cash or credit. Additionally, many patients secure funding via loans from family, friends, and/or institutions specializing in addiction financing. 

 Sliding-fee scales and payment plans . Some PHPs will adjust the cost of treatment for qualified individuals based on their incomes, or they may offer payment plans that allow you to pay for treatment over time. 

 Low cost/free rehabs. Various counties and states offer low-cost or free treatment for those with special circumstances or limited income. 

 Local, state, or federal funding. Many organizations such as SAMHSA provide grants and scholarships for treatment. 

 

 With facilities scattered across the country, American Addiction Centers (AAC) offers a host of PHP payment options. 

 How to Find a Partial Hospitalization Program Near You 

 The U.S. is home to more than 1,900 PHPs, including several facilities under the American Addiction Centers umbrella. 8   

 Contact American Addiction Centers at (629) 302-1607 to find a PHP near you. Our admissions navigators are available 24/7 to answer your confidential treatment questions, verify your insurance, explore payment options, and help you take your first steps toward recovery today.

		pointer-events: none;

	#nprogress .bar {
		background: #337fbd;

		position: fixed;
		z-index: 1031;
		top: 0;
		left: 0;

		width: 100%;
		height: 2px;
	}

	/* Fancy blur effect */
	#nprogress .peg {
		display: block;
		position: absolute;
		right: 0px;
		width: 100px;
		height: 100%;
		box-shadow: 0 0 10px #337fbd, 0 0 5px #337fbd;
		opacity: 1;

		-webkit-transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
		-ms-transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
		transform: rotate(3deg) translate(0px, -4px);
	}

	/* Remove these to get rid of the spinner */
	#nprogress .spinner {
		display: block;
		position: fixed;
		z-index: 1031;
		top: 15px;
		right: 15px;
	}

	#nprogress .spinner-icon {
		width: 18px;
		height: 18px;
		box-sizing: border-box;

		border: solid 2px transparent;
		border-top-color: #29d;
		border-left-color: #29d;
		border-radius: 50%;

		-webkit-animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
		animation: nprogress-spinner 400ms linear infinite;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent {
		overflow: hidden;
		position: relative;
	}

	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .spinner,
	.nprogress-custom-parent #nprogress .bar {
		position: absolute;

	}
	@keyframes nprogress-spinner {

jsx-726436161 content"><div id="wordpress-content" class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div class="jsx-1559800555 toc__container"><div class="sc-ae1ffb5e-0 emMzuR"><div></div></div></div><div class="jsx-1559800555"><div><div class="c-content-overview"><p>
Professional substance abuse treatment is instrumental in moving someone from a state of addiction and physical dependence towards a place of recovery and wellness.<sup>1</sup> Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) represent an important level of care on the continuum of treatment options.</p>
</div>
<p>As with any treatment for a medical or mental health issue, cost is a major concern for many people, if not the biggest concern. Before considering the cost of an IOP, remember that any fee for addiction treatment is only a general figure and the specific cost will be unique to the facility. The cost for one day of IOP treatment often ranges from $250 to $350 per day.<sup>2</sup> The cost of the program you attend could fall outside this range, however.</p>
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d2cdc"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>\nThe most influential factors affecting the cost of substance abuse programs are:<sup>3<\/sup><\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>Types of services offered.<\/li>\n<li>Intensity of services offered.<\/li>\n<li>Duration of treatment.<\/li>\n<li>Types of population treated, for example dual diagnosis, veterans, pregnant women, or people with significant medical concerns.<\/li>\n<li>Location of the treatment center and the amenities provided.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<p></p><div class="portal_media" id="portal_media-0"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"image":"https:\/\/admin.americanaddictioncenters.org\/wp-content\/uploads\/2018\/12\/calculating-the-cost.jpg","position":"left","alt":"cost calculating"},"children":""}</script>
	<style>
	#portal_media-0 {
		float: left;
		width: 50%;
		margin: 0 auto;
	}
	</style>
	</div>You can expect an IOP in a relatively less desirable location that offers standard treatment services to a general population to cost less than a program located in a beachside setting that offers a broad range of services from an expert staff. However, because your recovery is so important, when quality treatment is needed, it might not be the best time to focus on cost cutting.
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d2d52"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>\nCosts in the range of $250 to $350 per day might seem hard to swallow, but other more intensive forms of addiction treatment are significantly more expensive\u2014medical detoxes, inpatient rehabs, and partial hospitalization programs (PHPs) can cost hundreds more per day than IOPs:<sup>2<\/sup><\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>Medical detoxes \u2013 $500 – $650 per day.<\/li>\n<li>Inpatient treatment \u2013 $500 – $650 per day.<\/li>\n<li>Partial hospitalization programs (<a href=\"https:\/\/americanaddictioncenters.org\/rehab-guide\/partial-hospitalization-programs\">PHPs<\/a>) \u2013 $350 – $450 per day.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<p>Each level of care has its place in recovery. <a href="https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free">Though it may be tempting to seek the treatment with the lowest cost</a>, it is more important to find the services that meet your needs at the current time.<sup>4</sup></p>
<div class="portal_cta" id="portal_cta-69a74e20d2d60"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"button":"Learn More","link":"https:\/\/addiction-telehealth.com\/","position":"middle"},"children":"American Addiction Centers, is also offering telehealth services from the comfort of your home "}</script></div>
<div class="portal_ssvob" id="portal_ssvob-69a74e20d2d79"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{}}</script></div>
<h2>What You’re Paying For</h2>
<p>Since IOPs are all different, there will be some treatment variation from program to program. As an informed consumer, you’ll want to see where your money is going and if the treatment feels like the right fit for you.</p>
<div class="portal_quote" id="portal_quote-69a74e20d2d8d"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>Usually, IOPs consist of 9 hours of treatment per week, which is usually divided into 3-hour days of treatment occurring 3 times per week, but treatment could also take place anywhere from 2 to 5 days per week.<sup>5<\/sup><\/p>\n"}</script></div>
<p><a href="/therapy-treatment/group-individual">Group therapy is often the primary form of treatment</a> within an IOP, with individual and family sessions also available.</p>
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d2de9"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>\nGroup therapy will:<sup>5<\/sup><\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>Help you build communication skills and practice socializing with others.<\/li>\n<li>Help to establish structure and consistency in your life, which may have felt very chaotic during active addiction.<\/li>\n<li>Create a setting where group members can encourage and support each other in recovery.<\/li>\n<li>Give you a chance to guide others as you progress in your recovery.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<p>Group sessions may take place in a variety of formats and cover different topics.</p>
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d2ea7"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"title":"Group Meetings","type":"complex"},"children":"<p>\nTypes of group meetings include:<sup>5<\/sup><\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>Psychoeducational groups. Often utilized at the beginning of treatment, these groups provide education on drug use and its outcomes. Many of these groups will appear as lectures, with the group leader showing videos or speaking for a majority of the time.<\/li>\n<li>Skills groups. These groups focus on teaching new life skills and encourage participants to practice within sessions. Some types of skills groups are:\n<ul>\n<li>Drug refusal training. Clients can act out and role-play situations where they are pressured to use and experiment with new ways to respond.<\/li>\n<li>Relapse prevention. Clients can identify their triggers and potential high-risk scenarios and establish safety plans to manage them in healthier ways.<\/li>\n<li>Assertiveness training. By learning the differences between passive, aggressive, and assertive communication styles, clients can start using assertive communication with their fellow group members.<\/li>\n<li>Stress management. Here, clients can learn strategies to lower their stress, which improves their ability to stay drug-free.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<\/li>\n<li>Support groups. In these less-structured group setting (e.g., 12-step meetings), participants are able to use feedback from other members to help them adjust unhealthy or distorted thinking patternsThey also help the individual learn to relate better to others and appropriately resolve conflicts.<\/li>\n<li>Process groups. Focused on issues particularly important to group members like trauma, abuse, sexual orientation, parenting, or legal issues, these groups usually begin during the later stages of an IOP.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<p>The educational groups may have a larger number of people in each session while the process groups will be smaller and consist of fewer than 10 clients.<sup>5</sup> </p><div class="portal_quote" id="portal_quote-69a74e20d2eca"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"position":"right"},"children":"<p>The total course of IOP treatment frequently runs from 12 to 16 weeks.<sup>5<\/sup><\/p>\n"}</script></div>
<p>Now that you know what you are paying for, you may be wondering whether IOPs really work. Fortunately, the answer is yes!</p>
<p>When compared to inpatient rehab, IOPs can be just as beneficial to the recovering person, resulting in outcomes that are comparable to higher levels of treatment.<sup>6 </sup></p>
<h2>What If I Can’t Afford an IOP?</h2>
<p>Even though IOPs are significantly cheaper than higher levels of care, you may still worry about whether you can afford to attend one. Before you decide it’s too expensive, spend some time exploring your options. You likely have more than you think.</p>
<p></p><div class="portal_media" id="portal_media-1"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"image":"https:\/\/admin.americanaddictioncenters.org\/wp-content\/uploads\/2018\/12\/researching-your-options.jpg","position":"left","alt":"payment options for recovery"},"children":""}</script>
	<style>
	#portal_media-1 {
		float: left;
		width: 50%;
		margin: 0 auto;
	}
	</style>
	</div>Insurance is the most obvious place to start when it comes to paying for treatment. Often, both private and public/government-funded (Medicare and Medicaid) insurance providers cover numerous mental health and substance abuse services, and as long as there is a medical necessity for IOP, it will likely be covered in some part by your plan.
<p>To avoid a surprise medical bill, call your insurance company directly by using the number on the back of your card to ask them directly how much they’ll cover and which treatment providers are in their network. You can also call specific programs to see if they accept your insurance and how they collect copays.</p>
<div class="portal_quote" id="portal_quote-69a74e20d2eea"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>If you do not currently have health insurance, you can visit HealthCare.gov to find out about your state\u2019s options.<sup>7<\/sup><\/p>\n"}</script></div>
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d2f3b"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>\nIf you are unable to get insurance or your plan doesn’t cover your care, you can explore other payment options like:<\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>Financing and\/or sliding scale payments directly from the treatment center.<\/li>\n<li>Healthcare loans.<\/li>\n<li>Asking friends and family for donations in person or through social media\/ crowdfunding websites.<\/li>\n<li>Scholarships or charity care from the IOP.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<p>A little bit of time spent investigating your options can help you get the treatment you need.</p>
<div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d2fd8"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<\/p>\n<h4>More Topics on Paying for Treatment<\/h4>\n<ul>\n<li><a href=\"\/insurance-coverage\">Health Insurance Coverage<\/a><\/li>\n<li><a href=\"\/drug-detox\/insurance\">Insurance Help for Detox<\/a><\/li>\n<li><a href=\"\/drug-detox\/cost\">How Much Is Detox?<\/a><\/li>\n<li><a href=\"https:\/\/americanaddictioncenters.org\/rehab-guide\/rehab-cost\">Cost for Alcohol Recovery<\/a><\/li>\n<li><a href=\"\/personal-finance-in-recovery\">Addressing Personal Finances<\/a><\/li>\n<li><a href=\"\/rehab-guide\/public-assistance\">Public Assistance Care<\/a><\/li>\n<li><a href=\"\/rehab-guide\/state-funded\">State Funded Rehabs<\/a><\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<h2>Is Standard Outpatient Cheaper?</h2>
<p>People who pursue more standard types of outpatient treatment offerings, which could include regularly scheduled individual therapy, group therapy, or a combination of both sessions may only have a time commitment of a few hours a week. People may continue on with standard outpatient therapy for years, as it best suits their recovery needs.<sup>5</sup> This form of outpatient care may include only individual therapy, only group therapy, or a combination of the two. Standard outpatient treatment is usually longer in duration than IOP and can continue for months or years.<sup>5</sup></p>
<p>As mentioned, the intensity of services is a major factor affecting cost. Because of this, standard outpatient therapy is often cheaper than IOP per week. </p><div class="portal_quote" id="portal_quote-69a74e20d2fef"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{"position":"left"},"children":"<p>Standard outpatient may only cost around $200 per week instead of the higher IOP fee.<sup>3<\/sup><\/p>\n"}</script></div>
<p>Standard outpatient is a fantastic form of care, but it may not be enough to manage all of your symptoms and treatment needs. Clients who receive the enhanced services (e.g., case management) provided in some IOPs have been shown to have better outcomes than those who only received standard outpatient therapy.<sup>5</sup></p>
<p>A professional evaluation from an addiction expert can help to establish which type of outpatient treatment is best for you at the current time.<br>
</p><div class="portal_callout" id="portal_callout-69a74e20d3080"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<p>\nDuring the assessment, the professional will ask questions related to:<sup>4<\/sup><\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>The history of your substance use including:\n<ul>\n<li>Substances used.<\/li>\n<li>Duration of use.<\/li>\n<li>Frequency and dosage of use.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<\/li>\n<li>Previous experiences in treatment.<\/li>\n<li>Issues with withdrawal in the past.<\/li>\n<li>The presence of co-occurring mental or physical health issues that could hinder recovery.<\/li>\n<li>Available transportation and your ability to attend your outpatient sessions.<\/li>\n<li>Current stressors, supports, and commitments in your life.<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>\n"}</script></div>
<p><a href="/intensive-outpatient-programs">Rather than thinking about choosing IOP</a> or standard outpatient, consider IOP <em>and</em> standard outpatient. People who successfully treat a higher level of care often step down to the next lower level to continue their positive trajectory. Standard outpatient is often the step down from IOP.</p>
<p>Try not to let the cost of services discourage you from asking for the help you need to finally achieve the recovery and lifestyle you deserve. At the end of the day, the benefits of a stable life in recovery far outweigh the monetary burden.</p>
<div class="portal_sources" id="portal_sources-69a74e20d3152"><script crossorigin type="application/json">{"props":{},"children":"<\/p>\n<ol>\n<li>National Institute on Drug Abuse. </script><script id="__NEXT_DATA__" type="application/json">{"props":{"pageProps":{"_sentryTraceData":"4e66b8f936794a1095975fb7d33ecfa2-bb29882136f8ae45-0","_sentryBaggage":"sentry-environment=production,sentry-release=odsUSnXHa7Xks8o0SB39f,sentry-public_key=96a2ce264cf34a8eb5bbd664e8a614e8,sentry-trace_id=4e66b8f936794a1095975fb7d33ecfa2,sentry-sample_rate=0.01,sentry-transaction=%2F%5B...pathname%5D,sentry-sampled=false","data":{"id":54872,"date":"2018-12-14T13:13:09","modified":"2024-04-01T12:56:59","slug":"cost","type":"page","link":"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/intensive-outpatient-programs/cost","title":{"rendered":"The Cost to Attend an IOP"},"content":{"rendered":"\u003cdiv\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"c-content-overview\"\u003e\u003cp\u003e\nProfessional substance abuse treatment is instrumental in moving someone from a state of addiction and physical dependence towards a place of recovery and wellness.\u003csup\u003e1\u003c/sup\u003e Intensive outpatient programs (IOPs) represent an important level of care on the continuum of treatment options.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAs with any treatment for a medical or mental health issue, cost is a major concern for many people, if not the biggest concern. Before considering the cost of an IOP, remember that any fee for addiction treatment is only a general figure and the specific cost will be unique to the facility. The cost for one day of IOP treatment often ranges from $250 to $350 per day.\u003csup\u003e2\u003c/sup\u003e The cost of the program you attend could fall outside this range, however.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d2cdc\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\\nThe most influential factors affecting the cost of substance abuse programs are:\u003csup\u003e3\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eTypes of services offered.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eIntensity of services offered.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eDuration of treatment.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eTypes of population treated, for example dual diagnosis, veterans, pregnant women, or people with significant medical concerns.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eLocation of the treatment center and the amenities provided.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_media\" id=\"portal_media-0\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"image\":\"https:\\/\\/admin.americanaddictioncenters.org\\/wp-content\\/uploads\\/2018\\/12\\/calculating-the-cost.jpg\",\"position\":\"left\",\"alt\":\"cost calculating\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\n\t\u003cstyle\u003e\n\t#portal_media-0 {\n\t\tfloat: left;\n\t\twidth: 50%;\n\t\tmargin: 0 auto;\n\t}\n\t\u003c/style\u003e\n\t\u003c/div\u003eYou can expect an IOP in a relatively less desirable location that offers standard treatment services to a general population to cost less than a program located in a beachside setting that offers a broad range of services from an expert staff. However, because your recovery is so important, when quality treatment is needed, it might not be the best time to focus on cost cutting.\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d2d52\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\\nCosts in the range of $250 to $350 per day might seem hard to swallow, but other more intensive forms of addiction treatment are significantly more expensive\\u2014medical detoxes, inpatient rehabs, and partial hospitalization programs (PHPs) can cost hundreds more per day than IOPs:\u003csup\u003e2\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eMedical detoxes \\u2013 $500 \u0026#8211; $650 per day.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eInpatient treatment \\u2013 $500 \u0026#8211; $650 per day.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003ePartial hospitalization programs (\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/partial-hospitalization-programs\\\"\u003ePHPs\u003c\\/a\u003e) \\u2013 $350 \u0026#8211; $450 per day.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eEach level of care has its place in recovery. \u003ca href=\"https://americanaddictioncenters.org/rehab-guide/free\"\u003eThough it may be tempting to seek the treatment with the lowest cost\u003c/a\u003e, it is more important to find the services that meet your needs at the current time.\u003csup\u003e4\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_cta\" id=\"portal_cta-69a74e20d2d60\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"button\":\"Learn More\",\"link\":\"https:\\/\\/addiction-telehealth.com\\/\",\"position\":\"middle\"},\"children\":\"American Addiction Centers, is also offering telehealth services from the comfort of your home \"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_ssvob\" id=\"portal_ssvob-69a74e20d2d79\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{}}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003eWhat You’re Paying For\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eSince IOPs are all different, there will be some treatment variation from program to program. As an informed consumer, you’ll want to see where your money is going and if the treatment feels like the right fit for you.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_quote\" id=\"portal_quote-69a74e20d2d8d\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003eUsually, IOPs consist of 9 hours of treatment per week, which is usually divided into 3-hour days of treatment occurring 3 times per week, but treatment could also take place anywhere from 2 to 5 days per week.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"/therapy-treatment/group-individual\"\u003eGroup therapy is often the primary form of treatment\u003c/a\u003e within an IOP, with individual and family sessions also available.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d2de9\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\\nGroup therapy will:\u003csup\u003e5\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eHelp you build communication skills and practice socializing with others.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eHelp to establish structure and consistency in your life, which may have felt very chaotic during active addiction.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eCreate a setting where group members can encourage and support each other in recovery.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eGive you a chance to guide others as you progress in your recovery.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eGroup sessions may take place in a variety of formats and cover different topics.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d2ea7\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"title\":\"Group Meetings\",\"type\":\"complex\"},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\\nTypes of group meetings include:\u003csup\u003e5\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003ePsychoeducational groups. Often utilized at the beginning of treatment, these groups provide education on drug use and its outcomes. Many of these groups will appear as lectures, with the group leader showing videos or speaking for a majority of the time.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSkills groups. These groups focus on teaching new life skills and encourage participants to practice within sessions. Some types of skills groups are:\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eDrug refusal training. Clients can act out and role-play situations where they are pressured to use and experiment with new ways to respond.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eRelapse prevention. Clients can identify their triggers and potential high-risk scenarios and establish safety plans to manage them in healthier ways.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eAssertiveness training. By learning the differences between passive, aggressive, and assertive communication styles, clients can start using assertive communication with their fellow group members.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eStress management. Here, clients can learn strategies to lower their stress, which improves their ability to stay drug-free.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSupport groups. In these less-structured group setting (e.g., 12-step meetings), participants are able to use feedback from other members to help them adjust unhealthy or distorted thinking patternsThey also help the individual learn to relate better to others and appropriately resolve conflicts.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eProcess groups. Focused on issues particularly important to group members like trauma, abuse, sexual orientation, parenting, or legal issues, these groups usually begin during the later stages of an IOP.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eThe educational groups may have a larger number of people in each session while the process groups will be smaller and consist of fewer than 10 clients.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e \u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_quote\" id=\"portal_quote-69a74e20d2eca\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"position\":\"right\"},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003eThe total course of IOP treatment frequently runs from 12 to 16 weeks.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eNow that you know what you are paying for, you may be wondering whether IOPs really work. Fortunately, the answer is yes!\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eWhen compared to inpatient rehab, IOPs can be just as beneficial to the recovering person, resulting in outcomes that are comparable to higher levels of treatment.\u003csup\u003e6 \u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003eWhat If I Can’t Afford an IOP?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eEven though IOPs are significantly cheaper than higher levels of care, you may still worry about whether you can afford to attend one. Before you decide it’s too expensive, spend some time exploring your options. You likely have more than you think.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_media\" id=\"portal_media-1\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"image\":\"https:\\/\\/admin.americanaddictioncenters.org\\/wp-content\\/uploads\\/2018\\/12\\/researching-your-options.jpg\",\"position\":\"left\",\"alt\":\"payment options for recovery\"},\"children\":\"\"}\u003c/script\u003e\n\t\u003cstyle\u003e\n\t#portal_media-1 {\n\t\tfloat: left;\n\t\twidth: 50%;\n\t\tmargin: 0 auto;\n\t}\n\t\u003c/style\u003e\n\t\u003c/div\u003eInsurance is the most obvious place to start when it comes to paying for treatment. Often, both private and public/government-funded (Medicare and Medicaid) insurance providers cover numerous mental health and substance abuse services, and as long as there is a medical necessity for IOP, it will likely be covered in some part by your plan.\n\u003cp\u003eTo avoid a surprise medical bill, call your insurance company directly by using the number on the back of your card to ask them directly how much they’ll cover and which treatment providers are in their network. You can also call specific programs to see if they accept your insurance and how they collect copays.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_quote\" id=\"portal_quote-69a74e20d2eea\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003eIf you do not currently have health insurance, you can visit HealthCare.gov to find out about your state\\u2019s options.\u003csup\u003e7\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d2f3b\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\\nIf you are unable to get insurance or your plan doesn\u0026#8217;t cover your care, you can explore other payment options like:\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eFinancing and\\/or sliding scale payments directly from the treatment center.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eHealthcare loans.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eAsking friends and family for donations in person or through social media\\/ crowdfunding websites.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eScholarships or charity care from the IOP.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eA little bit of time spent investigating your options can help you get the treatment you need.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d2fd8\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003ch4\u003eMore Topics on Paying for Treatment\u003c\\/h4\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"\\/insurance-coverage\\\"\u003eHealth Insurance Coverage\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"\\/drug-detox\\/insurance\\\"\u003eInsurance Help for Detox\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"\\/drug-detox\\/cost\\\"\u003eHow Much Is Detox?\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/americanaddictioncenters.org\\/rehab-guide\\/rehab-cost\\\"\u003eCost for Alcohol Recovery\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"\\/personal-finance-in-recovery\\\"\u003eAddressing Personal Finances\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"\\/rehab-guide\\/public-assistance\\\"\u003ePublic Assistance Care\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"\\/rehab-guide\\/state-funded\\\"\u003eState Funded Rehabs\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003ch2\u003eIs Standard Outpatient Cheaper?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp\u003ePeople who pursue more standard types of outpatient treatment offerings, which could include regularly scheduled individual therapy, group therapy, or a combination of both sessions may only have a time commitment of a few hours a week. People may continue on with standard outpatient therapy for years, as it best suits their recovery needs.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e This form of outpatient care may include only individual therapy, only group therapy, or a combination of the two. Standard outpatient treatment is usually longer in duration than IOP and can continue for months or years.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eAs mentioned, the intensity of services is a major factor affecting cost. Because of this, standard outpatient therapy is often cheaper than IOP per week. \u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_quote\" id=\"portal_quote-69a74e20d2fef\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{\"position\":\"left\"},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003eStandard outpatient may only cost around $200 per week instead of the higher IOP fee.\u003csup\u003e3\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eStandard outpatient is a fantastic form of care, but it may not be enough to manage all of your symptoms and treatment needs. Clients who receive the enhanced services (e.g., case management) provided in some IOPs have been shown to have better outcomes than those who only received standard outpatient therapy.\u003csup\u003e5\u003c/sup\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eA professional evaluation from an addiction expert can help to establish which type of outpatient treatment is best for you at the current time.\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003c/p\u003e\u003cdiv class=\"portal_callout\" id=\"portal_callout-69a74e20d3080\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003cp\u003e\\nDuring the assessment, the professional will ask questions related to:\u003csup\u003e4\u003c\\/sup\u003e\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eThe history of your substance use including:\\n\u003cul\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSubstances used.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eDuration of use.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eFrequency and dosage of use.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003ePrevious experiences in treatment.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eIssues with withdrawal in the past.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eThe presence of co-occurring mental or physical health issues that could hinder recovery.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eAvailable transportation and your ability to attend your outpatient sessions.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eCurrent stressors, supports, and commitments in your life.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003c\\/ul\u003e\\n\u003cp\u003e\\n\"}\u003c/script\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"/intensive-outpatient-programs\"\u003eRather than thinking about choosing IOP\u003c/a\u003e or standard outpatient, consider IOP \u003cem\u003eand\u003c/em\u003e standard outpatient. People who successfully treat a higher level of care often step down to the next lower level to continue their positive trajectory. Standard outpatient is often the step down from IOP.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003eTry not to let the cost of services discourage you from asking for the help you need to finally achieve the recovery and lifestyle you deserve. At the end of the day, the benefits of a stable life in recovery far outweigh the monetary burden.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"portal_sources\" id=\"portal_sources-69a74e20d3152\"\u003e\u003cscript crossorigin type=\"application/json\"\u003e{\"props\":{},\"children\":\"\u003c\\/p\u003e\\n\u003col\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eNational Institute on Drug Abuse. (2018). \u003cem\u003e\u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/nida.nih.gov\\/sites\\/default\\/files\\/675-principles-of-drug-addiction-treatment-a-research-based-guide-third-edition.pdf\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener noreferrer\\\" class=\\\"broken_link\\\"\u003ePrinciples of Drug Addiction Treatment: A Research-Based Guide.\u003c\\/a\u003e\u003c\\/em\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cem\u003eAmerican Addiction Centers, 2018.\u003c\\/em\u003e\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eFrench, M. T., Popovici, I., \u0026amp; Tapsell, L. (2008). \u003ca href=\\\"https:\\/\\/www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov\\/pmc\\/articles\\/PMC2614666\\/\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" rel=\\\"noopener noreferrer\\\"\u003eThe Economic Costs of Substance Abuse Treatment: Updated Estimates and Cost Bands for Program Assessment and Reimbursement\u003c\\/a\u003e. \u003cem\u003eJournal of Substance Abuse Treatment\u003c\\/em\u003e, \u003cem\u003e35\u003c\\/em\u003e(4), 462\\u2013469.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSubstance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration. (2015). \u003cem\u003eDetoxification and Substance Abuse Treatment\u003c\\/em\u003e.\u003c\\/li\u003e\\n\u003cli\u003eSubstance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration.
